Prayer is the Original Spirit of Jihad: First, from this, the greatness and importance of prayer in religion is clarified. we have already discussed the mutual outward and inward relationship between prayer and Jihad under verse 77 of this Surah. Here the reality comes forward that prayer is something that cannot be ignored even within the dangers of war. The reason is that in Islam, war is not for bloodshed and looting but, as clarified in other places, to end that oppression and coercion which Allah's enemies establish to stop Allah's servants from worshipping Him. From this perspective, the original spirit of Jihad is prayer itself. It is through this that Jihad becomes the worship of Allah. If this spirit is not within it, then it too is 'Fasad fil-Ardh' (corruption in the land) just as every war of Allah's rebels is corruption. The mandatory demand of protecting this spirit is that even in the battlefield, prayer is not neglected as much as possible, so that every Mujahid is repeatedly reminded of the reality that his ranks in the battlefield are not different from his ranks in prayer in terms of their original purpose.

Importance of Congregational Prayer: Second, from this, the importance of congregational prayer is clarified. It has been stated under 'fa-in khiftum fa-rijalan aw rukbana' in verse 239 of Surah Al-Baqarah that during fighting, if circumstances are very perilous and performing prayer according to its etiquettes is impossible, then it should be performed in whatever state possible—riding, walking, standing, sitting, running—to the extent that even facing the Qibla is not mandatory. But along with all these concessions, the reality emerges from the verses under discussion that if the arrangement for congregational prayer is possible, its arrangement should be maintained even in the battlefield. Consequently, the Quran described a form for it that achieves both the purpose of congregational prayer and of defense.

Importance of Defense: Third, from this, the importance of defense is clarified. First, the fact that Allah Almighty granted a reduction in such an important worship as prayer for its sake is sufficient to clarify its importance. (Note that the concession of 'Qasr' [shortening] in prayer was originally revealed in connection with the journey of Jihad. In other journeys, its status is not original but, as clear from a statement of the Prophet, is like a charity from Allah Almighty. We will discuss this later). Second, the form of prayer described respects the full arrangement that the enemy is given no opportunity to take advantage of the Muslims' preoccupation with prayer to launch an attack. Effectively, the command of 'khudhu hidhrakum' (take your precautions) given at the start of this subject of Jihad was fully maintained even in prayer. This gives an idea of what a moderate and beautiful combination of trust and strategy, courage and wisdom, and valor and caution exists in this religion of nature, such that even prayer becomes Jihad.

Importance of Following the Prophet (PBUH): Fourth, from this, the passion for following the Prophet and its importance is expressed. Further on, we will clarify that the reason for this specific form of prayer described here is that in the presence of the Prophet (PBUH) in the battlefield, it was not possible for the Companions that the Prophet lead a congregational prayer and any Muslim be willing to be deprived of participating in that congregation. Since this passion of the Companions was natural, and the importance of this passion in religion is perfectly clear, Allah Almighty described a form of prayer that both encourages this passion and causes no harm to the objective of defense. In this light, recite the following verses.

'wa idha dharabtum fil-ardhi fa-laysa 'alaykum junahun an taqshuru minash-shalati in khiftum ay-yaftinakumul-ladhina kafaru innal-kafirina kanu lakum 'aduwwam-mubina'.

Permission for 'Qasr' in Prayer in relation to Defense: We hinted above that this permission for 'Qasr' (shortening) in prayer was revealed in connection with the command 'khudhu hidhrakum'. When the command was given to be armed with defensive equipment and ready for the encounter with the disbelievers, the question automatically arose: what will be the form of reconciling this command with prayer? Because the requirements of defense cannot be fulfilled in the state of prayer. For this, first, permission for 'Qasr' in prayer occurred, and in the following verses, the forms to be adopted in circumstances of congregational prayer, illness, and rain, etc., were described. The form of 'Qasr' proven from the practical continuity (tawatur) of the Prophet (PBUH) and the Companions is that prayers of four units (rak'ahs) are prayed as two. There is no 'Qasr' in Maghrib and Fajr.

Permission for 'Qasr' is a Concession (Rukhsat): From the words 'fa-laysa 'alaykum junahun' (there is no blame upon you), the point emerges that this permission for 'Qasr' is a type of concession. We clarified in a specific section in the exegesis of Surah Al-Baqarah regarding concessions that considering benefiting from them as contrary to Taqwa indicates a tendency toward extremism and excess in religion, which has been deemed reprehensible in both the Quran and Hadith. But simultaneously, it cannot be considered free from excess to give a concession the status of 'Azimat' (resolve) and 'Wujub' (obligation) such that its violation necessitates sin. Those seeking detail in this chapter should certainly cast a glance at the aforementioned section.

Permission for 'Qasr' is not specific only to the Journey of Jihad: There is no doubt that this permission for 'Qasr' was revealed in connection with the journey of Jihad, but it does not follow that it is specific only to the journey of Jihad. Any journey involves, to an extent, lack of tranquility, scramble, and concern for equipment. The only difference is that it is less in some and more in others; it is entirely possible that a journey of Jihad passes with more tranquility while a journey of trade or Hajj faces more confusions. Due to this shared cause ('illat), other journeys also fall under this ruling secondarily, if not originally. Consequently, on this basis, the Prophet (PBUH) gave permission for 'Qasr' in other types of journeys too. He practiced it himself and the Companions practiced it.

It should also be noted here that the words used here are 'wa idha dharabtum fil-ardh' (and when you travel through the land), which are general for every journey. There is no specification of the journey of Jihad in them. For the journey of Jihad, the specific phrase is 'idha dharabtum fi sabilillah' (when you travel in the path of Allah), which passed in verse 94. For this reason, the demand of the words is that the permission for 'Qasr' be general for every journey. As for the condition 'in khiftum' (if you fear) following it, it merely expresses the cause due to which this permission was granted in terms of the verse's occasion of revelation. From this, the point certainly emerges that this concession is nonetheless a concession subordinate to circumstances, but the point does not emerge that it is specific only to the journey of Jihad. Almost the same situation exists in the issue of polygamy, the discussion of which passed at the start of the Surah. Cast a glance at that too.

'wa idha kunta fihim fa-aqamta lahumush-shalata fal-taqum ta'ifatum-minhum-ma'aka wal-ya'khudhu ashlihatahum fa-idha sajadu fal-yakunu miw-wara'ikum wal-ta'ti ta'ifatun ukhra lam yushallu fal-yushallu ma'aka wal-ya'khudhu hidhrahum wa ashlihatahum waddal-ladhina kafaru law taghfuluna 'an ashlihatikum wa amti'atikum fa-yamiluna 'alaykum maylataw-wahidah wa la junaha 'alaykum in kana bikum adham-mim-matarin aw kuntum-mardha an tadha'u ashlihatakum wa khudhu hidhrakum innallaha a'adda lil-kafirina 'adhabam-muhina'.

Meaning of 'Hidhr': The word 'Hidhr' was discussed under verse 71. When used alone, it can refer to all types of weapons, whether of a purely defensive and protective nature like a shield, helmet, and armor, or of an aggressive nature like a sword and gun. But when used alongside the word 'Aslihah' (weapons), as in the verse under discussion—'an tadha'u ashlihatakum wa khudhu hidhrakum' (that you lay down your weapons but take your precautions)—it refers only to those things a soldier uses for protection from their enemy.

Reconciliation between the demands of Defense and Congregational Prayer when the Prophet (PBUH) leads: This verse describes the form that can be adopted for congregational prayer in the battlefield. In the presence of the Prophet (PBUH) in the battlefield, one difficulty likely to arise was that in the event of the possibility of establishing a congregation, when the Prophet stood to lead the prayer, every soldier would desire to pray behind you (PBUH) alone. This desire was a natural one, the consideration of which was also necessary, and simultaneously the arrangement of defensive strategies was indispensable so that the enemy would not take advantage of the Muslims' preoccupation with prayer to launch a sudden attack. Respecting both these demands, the strategy for establishing the congregation told was: one group stands for prayer behind the Imam with weapons, another group performs the duty of protection; when the first group has prostrated, they move back to take up the work of protection and monitoring, and the second group, which has not prayed, stands for prayer behind the Imam in that same armed state.

In this form, the demands of all three—establishing congregational prayer, following the Prophet (PBUH), and defense—are fulfilled. But what will be the number of units (rak'ahs) for the followers and the Imam? The answer to this question is not fully clear from this verse, due to which the opinions of jurists differed in this chapter, the detail of which is present in the books of Fiqh. For us, there is neither room to present all this detail here nor much need for it, because this form, as we hinted, was told for the solution of the difficulty that could arise in the presence of the Prophet (PBUH). After the Prophet, the desire to follow any single Imam would neither be so intense nor is its importance so great; therefore, according to the demands of defense, the members of the army can perform prayer behind different Imams.

The form of 'Salat al-Khawf' in the light of verse 102: What emerges from the words of the verse is: the Imam performs two units of 'Qasr' prayer, and both groups of followers perform one unit behind the Imam and one unit on their own to complete their prayer. Before standing for the second unit, the Imam should wait long enough for the first congregation to finish its second unit briefly and move back, and for the second congregation to take its place. In this way, both the followers and the Imam will have two units each.

The opinion of some is that the Imam will pray four units and both groups of followers will follow him in two units each. In this form, it is problematic that the Imam will perform 'Itmam' (full prayer) while the followers perform 'Qasr'. Even though the permission for 'Qasr' is for the Imam just as it is for the followers. The circumstances of both Imam and followers are also exactly the same. Some say the Imam will perform two units and both groups of followers will perform one unit behind him and end their prayer. In this form, the followers' prayer becomes only one unit, even though no prayer is one unit even in 'Qasr'. One reason for our inclination [toward the first view] is that in this way there will be perfect harmony in the prayer of both Imam and followers, both in terms of intention and outward form. Second reason is that from the words 'fa-idha sajadu' (then when they have prostrated) in the verse, the hint emerges that the followers must also perform one unit on their own. If this unit were not to be performed, or were to be performed only behind the Imam, then in place of 'fa-idha sajadu', the words 'fa-idha sajadta' (when you [singular] have prostrated) would have been used. There is no need to clarify that 'prostration' (sujud) is in reality an expression for the 'unit' (rak'ah), because the unit is completed only by prostration.

This Specific Form related to the Prophet's (PBUH) Presence: We suffice with this hint here. we do not want to go into more detail because in our view, the form of congregational 'Salat al-Khawf' is not necessarily this in every state and every era; rather, its connection, as we submitted, was specifically with the presence of the Prophet (PBUH). The explicit statement of this exists in the words of the verse: 'wa idha kunta fihim fa-aqamta lahumush-shalata' (And when you are among them and lead them in prayer). Obviously, this address is to the Holy Prophet (PBUH) alone. Addressing you, it is being stated that when you are present and stand to lead the people in congregational prayer, then this form should be adopted. The need for adopting this form in the Prophet's presence was the same one to which we hinted: that every person could also obtain the reward of following you (PBUH) and the objective of defense would not suffer any harm. In this era, first of all, the form of war has entirely changed. Second, the question of the Prophet's presence does not arise. For this reason, whatever form of establishing a congregation is adopted according to the demands of circumstances can be adopted, and if establishing a congregation is not possible, it can be prayed however possible.

'waddal-ladhina kafaru...' (Those who disbelieve wish...): this is the reason stated for this intensity of the arrangement for defense in the state of prayer. From this, it is clearly manifest that in Islam, the permission to be indifferent toward the demands of caution is not given in any state. To the extent that if forced to lay down weapons due to the distress of illness or rain, etc., one can only lay down the weapons. Regarding 'Hidhr'—meaning things of a protective nature—the permission to be indifferent still does not exist. 'innallaha a'adda lil-kafirina 'adhabam-muhina'. In this segment of the verse, it was clarified: as far as you are concerned, remain fully prepared for the crushing of these disbelievers. Anyway, Allah has already prepared a humiliating punishment for them; the point was said for the encouragement of the believers.

'fa-idha qadaytumush-shalata fadhkurullaha qiyamaw-wa qu'udaw-wa 'ala junubikum fa-idhat-ma'nantum fa-aqimush-shalata innash-shalata kanat 'alal-mu'minina kitabam-mawquta'.

Since a reduction occurred in the outward form of prayer due to the permission for 'Qasr', the instruction was given to be more active in the arrangement of its original reality—the remembrance of Allah—after finishing the prayer, so that this lack is compensated for and the permanence of Allah's remembrance, which is the soul of religion, is reminded. Especially in the battlefield, it has specific importance because the source of all resolve and morale is in reality the remembrance of Allah Almighty. After the state of fear departs and the state of peace and tranquility returns, the command for 'iqamat-e-salat' (establishing prayer) will immediately return. Meaning: the full prayer must be performed with congregation and adherence to time. From this verse, first, it is clarified that adherence to times is among the conditions of 'iqamat-e-salat'. Second, the point emerges that whatever the Prophet (PBUH) made mandatory upon the believers is the very duty appointed by Allah Almighty. This point emerges because it was stated regarding the prayers that they are mandatory with the arrangement of times, whereas the times of prayer are entirely those appointed by the Prophet (PBUH); no explicit detail of them exists in the Quran. At most, we can say there are some hints.

'wa la tahinu fi-btigha'il-qawmi in takunu ta'lamuna fa-innahum ya'lamuna kama ta'lamuna wa tarjuna minallahi ma la yarjuna wa kanallahu 'aliman hakima'.

When the word "Al-Qawm" appears in this context, it refers to the enemy and rival. Examples of this specific usage are many in Arabic literature. An example exists in the Quran too: 'If a wound has touched you, be sure a similar wound has touched the [other] people [al-qawm].'

Further Emphasis on the Subject of Urging Jihad: This is a further emphasis on the subject of urging Jihad that has been continuing from above; in fact, the mention of 'Salat al-Khawf' here is also, as we clarified, a link in this same series. Now it stated: if you suffer some loss at the hands of the enemy, you should not become low-spirited in pursuing them. Loss reaches them just as it reaches you. In this regard, you and they are equal; as for the success of the final end, in both this world and the Hereafter, it is a hundred percent yours alone; they have no share in it at all; so why be low-spirited by this temporary and provisional loss? Remember that Allah is Knowing and Wise. If He puts those performing Jihad in His path into some trial, or some loss reaches them, this thing is a demand of His knowledge and wisdom, through which the reform and training of the believers is intended.

Subject of Verses 105 to 115: Further on, addressing the Prophet (PBUH), the instruction was given: the Book Allah has granted you is now the touchstone of truth and falsehood; you deal with people by testing them on this touchstone; those who pass it are the believers and sincere ones, and those who prove false on it are the covenant-breakers and traitors; do not become their intercessor and advocate in the presence of God. God does not like such covenant-breakers and sinners. The point to which He hinted summarily in verse 88—'fa-ma lakum fil-munafiqina...'—its detail has now arrived. It passed there that among the Muslims were some people who held a very soft corner in their hearts for the hypocrites. They would try to give them concessions rightly or wrongly and sometimes would even declare them excused in matters where no room at all for excusing them existed. Although this attitude may be due to the softness of nature, declaring those excused whom God's Book does not declare excused and supporting them is synonymous with encouraging—rather, nurturing—hypocrisy. Consequently, here He first stopped the Prophet (PBUH) himself from this thing. This address toward the Prophet (PBUH) is like those whose examples have passed more than once in this book, and we clarified that in them, the direction of the address is toward the Prophet but the rebuke hidden within them is directed toward those people who are committing the error.

After addressing the Holy Prophet (PBUH), He directly addressed those Muslims who were supporting these hypocrites: you are supporting them in this world, but who will support them in the Hereafter? Following this, He stated: the correct method is neither to support one's error nor for someone to try to load their burden upon some other innocent person when they are seized, but rather it is repentance and seeking forgiveness. This warning was given because the hypocrites, first of all, would not admit any error of theirs; and if some error was seized such that no path for escaping responsibility was visible to them, they would try to thrust it upon the head of some innocent person through lies and slander. After that, addressing the Prophet (PBUH), He stated: it is merely Allah's favor and kindness and the blessing of the Book and Wisdom He granted that you remained protected from the Fitnas and machinations of these hypocrites; otherwise, they spared no effort in the attempt to turn you away from the path of truth. After that, He warned the hypocrites regarding their corrupt activities: those who, after the truth has become clear, are showing these activities in enmity of the Messenger and opposition to the way of the Muslims—Allah will turn them toward the same path they have chosen for themselves, and this path is the path of Hell. In this light, recite the following verses.

105-108: 'inna anzalna ilaykal-kitaba bil-haqqi li-tahkuma baynan-nasi bi-ma ara-kallahu wa la takul-lil-kha'inina khashiman. wastagh-firillaha innallaha kana ghafurar-rahima. wa la tujadil 'anil-ladhina yakhtanuna anfusahum innallaha la yuhibbu man kana khawwanan athima. yastakhfuna minan-nasi wa la yastakhfuna minallahi wa huwa ma'ahum idh yubayyituna ma la yardha minal-qawli wa kanallahu bi-ma ya'maluna muhita'.

Meaning of "Ira'at": The word 'Ira'at' (showing) in the Quran has come for that revelation which occurs to prophets in visions, as in Al-Anfal verse 43, and also for the guidance that occurs through the recited revelation, as in this verse. Since this word means 'to show', it contains a hint toward the reality that the guidance the Prophet receives through revelation is effectively the observation of realities with the eye of the head; for this reason, it is not possible for him to adopt any path apart from it. This subject is also coming in verse 113.

The word 'Mujadalah' in both Good and Bad meanings: The word 'Mujadalah' (dispute/advocacy) has come in both good and bad meanings in the Quran. Its meaning is debate, hair-splitting, and quarreling, but also complaining to someone on the basis of trust and self-abasement and interceding in someone's favor with persistence and entreaty. Abraham's 'mujadalah' regarding the people of Lot and the 'mujadalah' mentioned in Surah Al-Mujadilah are of this latter type.

Hypocrisy is Betrayal of one's own Conscience: 'alladhina yakhtanuna anfusahum' (those who betray themselves) refers to the hypocrites, who were mentioned with the word 'kha'inin' (traitors). 'Khiyanat' (betrayal) is a well-known word for that disloyalty and treachery a wife commits against her husband. Just as a disloyal woman gives herself into the bond of marriage to one man but extends the swings of love toward another, similarly the hypocrites make the covenant of obedience and loyalty with Allah and the Messenger but fill their breath with others. Regarding this betrayal of theirs, it was stated: they are betraying their own selves, because from this betrayal of theirs, nothing of God and the Messenger is ruined; what is ruined is their own selves, but they do not see it. Furthermore, it contains the aspect that they are criminals in the eyes of their own conscience. They know what they are doing in front and what they are doing behind the back; to be the advocate for such people is to become the referent of "the claimant is slow, the witness is fast."

Address to the Prophet, Rebuke to others: In 'wa la takun', 'wastagh-firillah', and 'wa la tujadil', the address is apparently to the Holy Prophet (PBUH), but the rebuke in it is directed toward those Muslims who were supporting the hypocrites. Such addresses, as we clarified in multiple places in this book, are to the Holy Prophet (PBUH) in his capacity as the representative of the Ummah. The point is said addressing you, but the objective is that those committing these acts cease them. This style also contains the eloquence that a kind of indifference and disregard is expressed toward those for whom the rebuke is intended. As if they are not worthy of address; therefore, Allah said the point He had to say by addressing His Messenger. In the addresses of this type that have appeared in the Holy Quran, generally the original direction of the address—as to who is actually being addressed—has also become clear from the gradual progression of the discourse. Consequently, here too the next verse 'ha-antum ha'ula'i jadaltum 'anhum...' clarified who is being pointed to. In the style of 'innallaha la yuhibbu...' (Indeed, Allah does not love...), there is an expression of the reality that the standard of liking and disliking for Allah, the Messenger, and the believers cannot be separate. How is it possible that the Messenger and the believers like those people of attributes and character whom Allah does not like? If there are some people who claim faith yet simultaneously like those who are disliked in the sight of Allah, then let them think for themselves where this point of theirs leads.

After clarifying the components of the speech, the aim of the verses is no longer in need of much explanation. The meaning is: We have revealed the Book with the truth; therefore, the touchstone for distinguishing between truth and falsehood is now this; you must decide between people by testing them on this touchstone as to who is on the truth and who is on falsehood, who is sincere and who is a hypocrite. This is the light given by Allah and the path shown by Him, after which no room remains for you to wander. For this reason, do not become the supporters and advocates of those who are committing breach of trust and betrayal with Allah and the Messenger. You seek forgiveness from Allah; Allah is very Forgiving and Merciful. Those committing breach of trust with Allah are not ruining anything of Allah but are committing breach of trust with their own souls and are criminals of their own hearts and consciences. God does not like such covenant-breakers and right-deprivers. So why should you defend those who are themselves criminals in the court of their own conscience and odious to God? It stated: they hide from humans and whisper against Allah and the Messenger in secret, but where can they hide from that Allah who is with them even at the time they are busy in their disliked secret consultations? Allah's knowledge encompasses everything. 

The mention of these hypocrites' hidden conspiracies and whisperings also passed in verse 81 of this Surah. See there too. Detailed discussion on this will occur in Surah Al-Mujadilah, God willing.

109-112: 'ha-antum ha'ula'i jadaltum 'anhum fil-hayatid-dunya fa-may-yujadilullaha 'anhum yawmal-qiyamati am-may-yakunu 'alayhim wakila. wa may-ya'mal su'an aw yazhlim nafsahu thumma yastagh-firillaha yajidillaha ghafurar-rahima. wa may-yaksib ithman fa-innama yaksibuhu 'ala nafsih wa kanallahu 'aliman hakima. wa may-yaksib khatî'atan aw ithman thumma yarmi bihi bari'an faqadihtamala buhtanaw-wa ithmam-mubina'.

'Ha' is a particle of alerting in Arabic; it was discussed elsewhere. It comes at the start of a sentence, and the objective is that the addressee listens to the point with open ears. The word 'Wakil' (Trustee/Advocate) when accompanied by 'ala' comes in three meanings depending on the occasion. 1. In the meaning of 'the one responsible and accountable.' For example: '...and We have not made you over them a guardian [hafizha], nor are you over them a manager [wakil]' (Al-An'am: 107). 2. In the meaning of 'monitor/overseer.' For example: '...He is the Creator of all things, so worship Him. And He is over all things Disposer [wakil].' 3. In the meaning of 'guarantor.' For example: '...Whichever of the two terms I complete, there shall be no injustice to me, and Allah, over what we say, is Witness [wakil].' Obviously, in 'am-may-yakunu 'alayhim wakila', it is in the first meaning. Meaning: today those supporting them can fight and argue in their support, but on the Day of Resurrection, when their case is presented in the court of the All-Knowing and Acquainted God, who will stand as the responsible one and the answerable one on their behalf? On that Day, they will nonetheless have to give the account themselves.

Now the point has opened clearly as to whose attitude was rebuked in the previous verses addressing the Prophet (PBUH). It was known that the addressee is not the Prophet (PBUH) personally, but those among the Muslims who periodically rolled up their sleeves in support of the hypocrites due to their personal relationships and tried to find some excuse for their acquittal despite their explicit errors. Consequently, addressing them, it was stated: O people! Listen with open ears that today you can fight and argue in their support, but tomorrow when their trial occurs with God, who will argue with God in their defense or who will become responsible on their behalf? Following this, He stated: the path to escaping God is not for others to stand as back-supports in defense of the criminal, but it is that if some evil or some 'oppression of the soul' (Shirk) is issued from someone, they should turn toward Allah and ask Him for forgiveness; whoever seeks forgiveness with sincerity will find Allah Forgiving and Merciful. With God, one will not carry the burden of another; whoever is guilty of a sin, its burden will fall upon them, because God is both Knowing and Wise. The demand of His knowledge and His justice and wisdom is that Zaid's account does not fall into Bakr's ledger; rather, everyone is themselves the answer for their own action. 'Every soul is a pledge for what it has earned.'

A Specific Weapon of Conspiratorial Groups: In 'wa may-yaksib khatî'atan...', another mischief of the hypocrites was unveiled: these people, when seized for some error or some deprivation of rights, adopt the path of lies and slander instead of admission and try to thrust its burden upon some innocent person. It stated: this path of acquittal from God is also wrong. The world can be deceived by this slander and lie; God cannot be deceived. With God, such criminals will not only carry the burden of their crime but will also add slander and lie to their original crime. It should be remembered here that the mention of the hypocrites' conspiracies and whisperings passed above. The specific weapon of conspiratorial groups for their defense is precisely this: when they are caught, they try to thrust the accusation for it upon other innocents or at least commit slander to involve them in it too, so as to lighten their own burden of sin.

'wa law la fadhlullahi 'alayka wa rahmatuhu lahammat-ta'ifatum-minhum ay-yudhilluka wa ma yudhilluna illa anfusahum wa ma yadhurrunaka min shay'in wa anzalallahu 'alaykal-kitaba wal-hikmata wa 'allamaka ma lam takun ta'lam wa kana fadhlullahi 'alayka 'azhima'.

This is a digression (Iltifat) toward the Prophet (PBUH) and a warning to the Muslims through your medium: it is Allah's special favor and kindness upon you that you remained protected from the evil of these hypocrites; otherwise, a group of theirs had the effort and conspiracy day and night to turn you away from the path; but Allah, through His special favor, granted you the light of the Book and Wisdom which kept you protected from slipping. In this is a warning to the Muslims to keep themselves protected from the calamities and Fitnas of this group, and simultaneously the strategy for remaining protected was told: the method is that they value with a sincere heart the bounty of the Book and Wisdom they have received and do not fall for the tricks of those who are carving out their own path apart from it. In 'wa ma yudhilluna illa anfusahum wa ma yadhurrunaka min shay'in' is the statement of a factual reality: those deviating from the path of truth, if they do not succeed in turning those on the path of truth away from the truth, then despite all their 'selling of wisdom' (pretension), they only mislead themselves; they cannot cause any harm to those established on the straight path. The meaning is: you continue watching from your stance of truth into what pit of destruction these people fall.

114-115: 'la khayra fi kathirim-min-najwahum illa man amara bi-shadaqatin aw ma'rufin aw islahim-baynan-nas wa may-yaf'al dhalika-btigha'a mardhatillahi fa-sawfa nu'tihi ajran 'azhima. wa may-yushaqiqir-rasula mim-ba'di ma tabayyana lahul-huda wa yattabi' ghayra sabilil-mu'minina nuwallihi ma tawalla wa nushlihi jahannam wa sa'at mashira'.

Meaning of 'Najwa' and the aspect of Good and Evil in it: 'Najwa' means to speak to someone in a whispering and secret manner. There is no evil in this in itself because there can be many occasions where the method of secrecy and whispering is in accord with benefit. It is not necessary to give the Adhan (call out loudly) in every place and every occasion. The evil or goodness in it is produced from the nature of the point. If the point is of goodness and Taqwa, then it is a 'Najwa of Good'; and if the point is of mischief and Fitna, then it is a 'Satanic Najwa.' The Quran clarified this reality in Surah Al-Mujadilah: 'O you who have believed, when you converse privately [tanajaytum], do not converse about sin and aggression... but converse about righteousness and piety.' In 'illa man amara bi-shadaqatin', a 'mudhaf' (possessive noun) is omitted, just as in 'wa lakinnal-birra man amana billah', which passed in Al-Baqarah. Meaning: their 'Najwa' is mostly satanic, but there is good in the 'Najwa' of those who whisper for charity, goodness, and reconciliation. Discussion on the words "Mushaqqah" (opposition) and "Al-Huda" (the Guidance) passed in Surah Al-Baqarah. "Mushaqqah" with the Messenger means trying to establish one's own separate party in opposition to the Messenger, and "Al-Huda" means "Huda Allah" (Allah's Guidance), just as "Al-Kitab" means the Book of Allah. In "sabilil-mu'minin" (the path of the believers), 'believers' refers to the Companions of the Messenger. The way of life they adopted was based entirely on Divine guidance; therefore, following it is the following of Allah and the Messenger. Carving out any path apart from it is misguidance. In "nuwallihi ma tawalla" (We will turn him to what he has turned to), the same style is respected as in 'when they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate'—meaning those who try to carve out a path apart from the path of the believers, God will leave them to turn toward that very path they wish to go upon. Regarding the abandonment or adoption of truth, there is no compulsion with God. Whoever wants to walk on God's path, Allah grants them His success; whoever wants to adopt some path they liked apart from it, He gives them respite for that too.

The Nature of the Hypocrites' Whispering: The hypocrites' whisperings and conspiracies, to which He hinted in verse 108 with the words 'when they spend the night plotting what He does not approve of statements'—now their clarification is occurring: that the majority of their whisperings are entirely empty of good and consist only of points of Fitna and corruption. A blessed whispering can only be that which is for the purpose of urging charity, encouraging goodness, and the objective of reconciling between people; and there is no doubt that those who work secretly for these high objectives for the sake of seeking God's pleasure—Allah will give them a great reward. After that, He stated: those who—after understanding well that the path of guidance to which the Messenger is calling is the guidance revealed by Allah—oppose the Messenger and want to adopt some other method while abandoning the method adopted by the believers, Allah will leave them to go upon their chosen path, and they will reach Hell directly through that path, which is a wretched destination. Reflecting on these two verses reveals that the Quran has, in an extremely eloquent style, both clarified the nature of these hypocrites' 'Najwa' and unveiled its objective. When He stated that there is no good in their 'Najwa'—that a 'Najwa of Good' is the 'Najwa' of those who urge toward charity, enjoining what is right, and trying for reconciliation—then from this, the point automatically emerged: these people, in the whisperings they perform, stop people from spending in the path of Allah, urge toward the 'Munkar' (evil) instead of the 'Ma'ruf' (good), and plot to create hypocrisy among the Muslims instead of reconciliation. Similarly, when He stated that those who oppose the Messenger and Allah's guidance after the guidance of Allah has become thoroughly clear, and try to carve out their own side-path apart from the path of the Muslims—Allah will leave them to go upon their liked path which will take them and drop them straight into Hell—then effectively He clarified: the aim of all these conspiracies and whisperings of these people is in reality to make their own separate party in opposition to the Messenger, to carve out their own separate track in opposition to 'Huda Allah', and to follow the way of ignorance in opposition to the way of the believers. When I read these verses, my mind repeatedly goes toward the point that this was the answer given to those people who used to argue and debate with the Messenger of Allah (PBUH) and the sincere believers in support of these hypocrites. Obviously, when information regarding these hypocrites' secret assemblies and hidden conspiracies reached the Prophet and the Companions, they must have been seized upon in some nature. At that time, these supporters of theirs, mentioned above, would have been saying in their defense: "These people are very sincere; they are great well-wishers of the nation; the points discussed in their assemblies are for the welfare and well-wishers of Islam and the Muslims." And if some such point came into the grasp in this series for which no answer could be formed, they would have been thrusting the accusation for it, as hinted above, upon some such decent person in whose margin of thought that point had never even occurred. In response to this advocacy of those people, the Quran unveiled those 'inside-the-house' whisperings in an extremely eloquent manner—and see how beautifully He unveiled them: the entire point came forward, and no room for any debate or refutation remained for the addressee.

Subject of Verses 116 to 126: Further on, first the reason is stated as to why these hypocrites—who want to carve out their own path apart from the path of the believers while opposing the Messenger after Allah's guidance has become clear—will fall into Hell. The answer given is: adopting any path against Allah's guidance, establishing one's own party against God's Messenger, and carving out a path apart from the believers' path is Shirk in its reality, and Allah Almighty's decision regarding Shirk is that He is not going to forgive it. 

The Reality of Shirk: Following this, in connection with the mention of Shirk, the reality of Shirk was clarified: those who commit Shirk are in reality followers of Satan. They have become trapped in the deceptive snares of desires he has spread for them, and they are fulfilling whatever he suggests to them like the blind and deaf, even though all his promises are pure deception. The resting place of these people is Hell, from which they will not be blessed with an exit after entering. 

Success in the Hereafter is only for the People of Tawheed: Then the success and felicity of the people of Tawheed was mentioned: these people will undoubtedly enter God's eternal Paradise, and Allah's promises for them are not mere deception like Satan's promises but are entirely reality, and whose word can be truer than God's word? 

The thing that is of use with God is faith and righteous action. Only those will enter God's Paradise who possess this commodity; those deprived of it will suffer the punishment for their evils, whoever they may be. 

No Religion is a greater Bearer of Tawheed than the Religion of Abraham: Finally, it stated: the original religion belongs to those who are followers of Islam. This is the religion of Abraham. Abraham was a worshipper of the One God, and it was due to this very Tawheed of his that God made him a friend. Every single thing in this universe belongs to God, and God is encompassing everything. In this light, recite the following verses.

'innallaha la yaghfiru ay-yushraka bihi wa yaghfiru ma duna dhalika limay-yasha' wa may-yushrik billahi faqad dhalla dhalalam-ba'ida'.

The word "Duna" (besides/less than) in its usage comes in the meanings of both 'this side' and 'that side', 'below' and 'above', and 'in front' and 'behind.' Context determines what is intended where. Here it is obviously in the meaning of 'this side' or 'below.' Just as the source of all good is Tawheed—meaning not associating anyone in God's Essence, Attributes, and His rights—similarly the source of all evil is Shirk—meaning associating a partner in God's Essence, Attributes, and His rights. While remaining upon Tawheed, if a human commits some stumble, it is accidental due to the dominance of the soul and emotions. It does not happen that they make their error their 'covering and bedding.' For this reason, they necessarily rise after falling. In contrast, if some good deed occurs from someone alongside Shirk, it is accidental, having no connection with the original source of good, i.e., God; therefore, it is foundationless. The polytheist, due to being cut off from God, necessarily hands over their reins to the soul and Satan; for this reason, they become so distant from the straight path stage by stage that no possibility remains for them to return toward God until they repent from Shirk. For this reason, there is no forgiveness for Shirk with God. However, if sins occur alongside Tawheed, Allah Almighty will forgive for whom He wills. The qualifier "for whom He wills" (limay-yasha') is also worth considering. Alongside the mention of the forgiveness of sins committed with faith, Allah Almighty also placed the qualifier "for whom He wills," which is an extremely important qualifier. We have mentioned repeatedly in this book that it is proven from the Quran without any doubt that no will of God is separate from His wisdom; therefore, this forgiveness will be obtained only by those who prove worthy of it under His justice and wisdom. This qualifier closed all doors of improper audacity in this matter. Allah Almighty has personally described in the Quran the rules and regulations He has kept for this will of His.

117-121: 'in yad'una min dunihi illa inathan wa in yad'una illa shaytanam-marida. la'anahullah wa qala la-attakhidhanna min 'ibadika nashibam-mafrudha. wa la-udhillannahum wa la-umanniyannahum wa la-amurannahum fa-layubattikunna adhanal-an'ami wa la-amurannahum fa-layughayyirunna khalqallah wa may-yattakhidhish-shaytana waliyyam-min dunillahi faqad khasira khusranam-mubina. ya'iduhum wa yumannihim wa ma ya'iduhumush-shaytanu illa ghurura. ula'ika ma'wahum jahannamu wa la yajiduna 'anha mahisha'.

"In yad'una" (They do not call): calling here refers to that calling which is for the purpose of prayer, plea, cry for aid, seeking help, seeking mercy, etc., in the sense in which a deity is called upon. 

"Inathan" refers to presumed Goddesses: 'Inathan' is the plural of 'Untha'. 'Untha', in its literal sense, is used for a soft and delicate and loose thing, but its well-known usage is for a woman. Here, since it is in the context of the mention of the polytheists' gods and goddesses, it refers to goddesses. It should be remembered that in the mythology of polytheists—regardless of which nation or country's polytheists they are—goddesses have held the greatest importance. This can be confirmed by casting a cursory glance at the history of the polytheistic religions of China, India, Arabia, Egypt, and Babylon/Nineveh, etc. It is also particularly to be noted that the original necessities of life have been considered related mostly to these goddesses. In the pagan Arabs too, the system of divinity was mostly occupied by goddesses. Lat, Manat, 'Uzza, etc., are the names of goddesses. These were, as will be clarified in the exegesis of Surah An-Najm, idols of angels regarding whom the polytheists held the creed that these are God's pampered and beloved daughters whose word God never refuses. For this reason, whatever is asked through their medium—if they are pleased—it is bound to be received. The reference to this creed of theirs is in various places in the Quran, for example: 'And they made the angels, who are servants of the Most Merciful, females [inathan].' Within the discussion of 'Jibt' and 'Taghut', we also clarified that the People of the Book too, when they encountered polytheistic nations, adopted many of their polytheistic methods and made many goddesses and gods for themselves like them, which their prophets lamented, and this lamentation exists in their own scriptures. The creed the Christians hold regarding Maryam is also known. 

Satan is the Imam of Shirk: "wa in yad'una illa shaytanam-marida" (and they call not except a rebellious Satan). The mention of calling Satan is in the capacity of the original mentor of Shirk, because it is he who committed face-to-face disobedience to God's command in the matter of prostrating to Adam; and when God cursed him for this rebellion and defiance, he threatened: "I will mislead Your servants through various methods and plunge them into Shirk." For this reason, wherever and in whatever form Shirk is found, its Imam is in reality Satan alone, and in this regard, every prayer and entreaty and every worship and obedience being performed to any other-than-God or for any other-than-God is indirectly to and for this Imam of Shirk alone. 

"La'anahullah" (Allah cursed him) in the sense of an incidental phrase: 'la'anahullah' can be an attribute because at the time Satan threatened to plunge Allah's servants into Shirk, Allah Almighty—as mentioned in the Quran—had also cursed him at that same time; therefore, he is the permanent possessor of this attribute. But I have taken it in the sense of an incidental phrase and translated it accordingly. The nuance of eloquence in this is that as soon as the name of this Imam of Shirk appeared, Allah Almighty cursed him right in the faces of his worshippers. This is the most effective method for expressing hatred.

Satan's Threat to the Children of Adam: 'wa qala la-attakhidhanna min 'ibadika nashibam-mafrudha' (and he said, "I will surely take from Your servants a specific portion"). This references the threat Satan gave at the time he openly disobeyed God's command regarding prostrating to Adam and God declared him an outcast. The Quran has mentioned this threat in multiple places. For example: '[Allah] said, "Descend from it, for it is not for you to be arrogant therein. So get out; indeed, you are of the debased." [Iblis] said, "Reprieve me until the Day they are resurrected." [Allah] said, "Indeed, you are of those reprieved." [Iblis] said, "Because You have put me in error, I will surely sit in wait for them on Your straight path. Then I will come to them from before them and from behind them and on their right and on their left, and You will not find most of them grateful [to You]." [Allah] said, "Get out of it, reproached and expelled. Whoever of them follows you - I will surely fill Hell with you, all together."' (Al-A'raf: 13-18).

From this debate of Satan, his zeal and activity for misleading the children of Adam is also fully expressed, and the point also manifests that the specific target of all his efforts of misguidance is the creed of Tawheed. Consequently, when he said "I will surely sit in wait for them on Your straight path," this straight path is that very path of Tawheed which the Quran described as 'Sirat al-Mustaqim'. And when he said "You will not find most of them grateful," this too is an expression for the point: "I will plunge them into Shirk and they will sing the praises of others instead of Your praise."

'Amani' (False Hopes): 'wa la-umanniyannahum' (and I will arouse in them [sinful] desires) points toward those types of false hopes in which polytheistic nations generally become afflicted. For example: the Arabs' creed that the goddesses and gods they worship intercede for them with God. Through their intercession, they receive the blessings of the world, and if the Hereafter is a thing, then through their intercession they will be rightful claimants of Paradise in the Hereafter too. Similarly, the Jews fell into the delusion that they are the children of Abraham, the Friend of Allah, and God's chosen servants; therefore, they hold the status of God's sons and beloveds and are hereditary rightful claimants of Paradise—whatever their deeds may be, the fire of Hell will first of all not touch them, and if it does, only temporarily. We discussed these false hopes of theirs under Al-Baqarah verses 80 and 111. By the same analogy, the Christians' creed that God has made His son the expiation for all their sins; now they are absolved of all responsibilities of action and obedience. 

Polytheistic Vows: 'fa-layubattikunna adhanal-an'ami': 'batak' means to cut, slit, or tear. There has been a shared tradition among polytheistic nations that they would leave specific animals as a vow in the name of their imaginary deities by slitting their ears. This slitting of ears was for the purpose that others would recognize them as a vow and not interfere with them. 

Meaning of 'Taghyir Khalq Allah' (Changing Allah's Creation): 'fa-layughayyirunna khalqallah' (and they will surely change the creation of Allah). Changing Allah's creation originally refers to changing that 'Fitrah' (nature) of Allah upon which Allah Almighty created all creation. For example: Tawheed is the religion of nature, but Satan and his agents distorted it with Shirk. In Surah Ar-Rum, while refuting Shirk, it was stated: 'So direct your face toward the religion, inclining to truth. [Adhere to] the fitrah of Allah upon which He has created [all] people. No change should there be in the creation of Allah. That is the correct religion, but most of the people do not know. [Adhere to it], turning in repentance to Him, and fear Him and establish prayer and do not be of those who associate others with Allah' (Ar-Rum: 30-31). In this verse, Tawheed was described as the religion of nature and the 'correct religion' (din al-qayyim), and the proof given was that He created people upon it. Then He stated that changing Allah's nature and creation is not permissible. In our view, this same thing is intended in the segment under discussion. Incidentally, all those things that fall under the ruling of changing Allah's nature will come under it. For example: women becoming men or men becoming women, or making women and men incapable of having children, and other absurdities of this nature. 

Various Meanings of the word 'Amr': Regarding Satan's 'commanding' (amr) mentioned in 'wa la-amurannahum', it should be remembered that in Arabic, the word 'Amr' comes in the meanings of commanding, suggesting, and advising. Satan ruins the path of Tawheed through all these methods. He plants whispers in hearts through his satanic inspiration, and he gives advice through those among Jinn and humans who become his agents; and if his agents are powerful and in authority, he has laws made through their hands and has those laws implemented too.

After the explanation of the components, now cast a glance at the system and meaning of these verses as a whole too. In the previous verse, the mention of Shirk being an unforgivable crime occurred, so He mentioned the weakness of Shirk and its 'lineage' while He was at it, so that its loathsome face is thoroughly unveiled. 

The Weakness of Shirk and its Lineage: The mention of the weakness was made from two aspects. One, that this entire machinery of Shirk is established on the strength of goddesses; first, it is the height of foolishness for a human to seek a support other than the One God, then it is foolishness upon foolishness that the support is of imaginary women whose helplessness and weakness is itself a proverb. Second, that its entire foundation is on the false hopes created by Satan and his deceptive promises, and all of Satan's promises are entirely reality-less. When the reality opens, it will be seen that neither Abraham's lineage is of any use, nor do Lat and Manat and their intercession have any existence; rather, the entire matter depends on faith and righteous action. Those who do not possess this commodity have only Hell, from which there is no path of escape. The statement of its lineage was made thus: its originator and Imam is the accursed Iblis who, in the surge of envy, gave the threat on the very first day: "I will surely take my portion from Your servants; I will mislead them, I will entrap them in various types of false hopes; they will present offerings to idols by my command and will distort Allah's nature by my inspiration." It stated: who can be more wretched and failed than those who make this accursed and rebellious Satan their reference point and designer? It stated: Satan is showing them the green gardens of promises and spreading the nets of hopes before them, even though all of Satan's promises are pure deception. Neither intercession will be of use to them nor affiliation with the elders. Their resting place is Hell, from which there will be no escape.

122-124: 'wal-ladhina amanu wa 'amilush-shalihati sanudkhiluhum jannatin tajri min tahtihal-anharu khalidina fiha abada wa'dallahi haqqa wa man ashdaqu minallahi qila. laysa bi-amaniyyikum wa la amaniyyi ahlil-kitab may-ya'mal su'ay-yujza bihi wa la yajid lahu min dunillahi waliyyaw-wa la nashira. wa may-ya'mal minash-shalihati min dhakarin aw untha wa huwa mu'minun fa-ula'ika yadkhulunal-jannata wa la yuzhlamuna naqira'.

The Path to Salvation is Faith and Righteous Action, not False Hopes: Meaning: Satan's promises and the hopes he produces are merely lies and deception. However, Allah's promise is that He will admit those who adopt the path of faith and righteous action into Paradise, and this promise is absolute truth because it is Allah's promise, and whose promise can be truer than Allah's? Then He stated: these false hopes regarding salvation—whether they be yours (the hint is toward the hypocrites) or the People of the Book's—none of them are going to be fulfilled. Whoever does evil will suffer the punishment for what they did, and no one besides God will be able to be their designer and helper. Similarly, whoever performs righteous action—whether male or female—if they are a believer, then these people will enter Paradise and not the slightest bit will be decreased from their reward. By mentioning the hopes of both the hypocrites and the People of the Book together, the Quran clarified: all those seeing dreams of Paradise on the strength of Shirk, intercession, and family-based chosenness are inhabitants of the same 'fools' paradise' and captives of the same net of deception, and the failure of all of them is of the same kind. The game of the Hereafter belongs to those who have adopted the path of faith and righteous action. Whoever possesses this wealth is successful, whether they be male or female, Israelite or Ishmaelite, Arab or non-Arab. In this way, no decrease will occur for anyone due to any affiliation.

125-126: 'wa man ahsanu dinam-mimman aslama wajhahu lillahi wa huwa muhsinuw-wattaba'a millata Ibrahima hanifa wattakhadhallahu Ibrahima khalila. wa lillahi ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ardh wa kanallahu bi-kulli shay'im-muhita'.

The Religion of Abraham: Now the authority and the rank and status of this 'Huda Allah' (Allah's Guidance) and 'Sabil al-Mu'minin' (the Path of the Believers) were clarified, whose opposition was declared Shirk and from which the above debate had arisen. The meaning is: what is the sense in opposing it and deviating from it? After all, whose religion can be better than the one who surrenders their entire self to Allah and is simultaneously a 'Muhsin'—meaning they fulfill every command of their Lord in the way it should be fulfilled? This is the religion of Abraham. Whoever adopted this path followed the religion of Abraham, and Abraham's person is the one whom God made His friend; so whose religion can be greater than his religion? Finally, the cause of 'aslama wajhahu lillah' (he surrendered his face to Allah) was clarified: whatever is in the heavens and the earth belongs to the One Allah alone, and He is also encompassing everything; so who else is more deserving that one should surrender themselves to Him?

The Reason for Prophet Abraham being 'Khalilullah' (the Friend of Allah): It is worth remembering that the mention of Prophet Abraham being 'Khalilullah' occurred repeatedly in the Torah too, and this thing is among those due to which the Children of Israel fell into the false desire: since they are from the family of God's friend, their status is that of 'sons of God' and 'beloveds of God', and when their status is this, what audacity does the fire of Hell have to touch them? To refute this delusion, it was also clarified here: Allah made Abraham His friend because he, cutting off from every direction and being entirely single-minded, surrendered himself completely to his Lord. His religion is the religion of Islam and Tawheed, and he is the Imam of this religion of Tawheed. For this reason, the true religion belongs to those who are followers of this Imam of Tawheed's religion, not those who are followers of the Imam of Shirk, Iblis.

Subject of Verses 127 to 134: The points that were fundamental regarding the establishment, organization, and purification of Islamic society concluded with the verses above. Now the following part, until the end of the Surah, holds the status of the conclusion of the Surah. In it, first, answers were given to some questions that arose later regarding the commands described in verses 2-4 of this Surah; after that, until the end of the Surah, advices of the nature of final warnings were given addressing the Muslims, the hypocrites, and the People of the Book. These questions arose later; for this reason, their answers were placed with the final chapter of the Surah so that it becomes clear they were revealed later. This provides guidance in understanding the wisdom of the commands. 

To understand the group of verses under discussion, cast another glance at verses 2-4: there, permission was given to marry the mothers of orphans from the perspective of the orphans' benefit and welfare, and it was accompanied by the limit of four and the conditions of paying Mahr and justice. It seems that questions arose among the people regarding both Mahr and justice. Regarding Mahr: that the restriction to pay Mahr to those women who are married solely for the benefit of their orphan children is a heavy hardship which the guardians will not be able to bear. Similarly, if the meaning of justice is perfect equality in both heart-felt inclination and outward treatment, then this too is impossible. If a person, while having a liked wife, married a woman solely with the thought that it would facilitate the training and monitoring of the rights of her orphan children, then how is it possible that he can love and treat his favorite wife and this second wife exactly the same? The Quran answered both these questions here. To the first question, the answer given was: if a person does not like a woman, why marry her at all? If marrying after liking her, then pay the Mahr. But simultaneously, He also clarified that the matter of Mahr is originally the woman's matter. If she, under her own benefit, makes some compromise with her husband, she has the authority for that and this is better. Anyway, the thing befitting a man is that instead of suppressing the suppressed, he adopts the path of excellence (Ihsan) and Taqwa. To the second question, the answer given was: justice does not mean that heart-felt inclination and outward treatment must be exactly equal like the weight on a scale. No one can perform this type of justice even if they want to with full sincerity. The thing required is that in outward treatment and transactions, the method remains such that the rights of both continue to be fulfilled; it should not be that one wife becomes entirely 'Mu'allaqah' (suspended)—obtaining neither the heart's love nor the outward treatment, being neither a wife nor a divorcee.

After that, He gave advice in a warning tone: whatever is in the heavens and the earth belongs to God. He had instructed the People of the Book too to observe His limits, and He is giving this same instruction to you too; if you observe them, you will build yourselves; if you disobey, you will not harm God at all. God is independent of all and Praiseworthy. If He wills, He can erase all of you and bless others in your place. He is powerful over everything. Those who become seekers of only the world receive from the world as much as is destined, and for those who become seekers of the Hereafter—God possesses the treasures of both the world and the Hereafter. In this light, recite the following verses.

'wa yastaftunaka fin-nisa'i qulillahu yuftikum fihinna wa ma yutla 'alaykum fil-kitabi fi yataman-nisa'il-lati la tu'tunahunna ma kutiba lahunna wa targhabuna an tankihuhunna wal-mustadh'afina minal-wildani wa an taqumu lil-yatama bil-qisti wa ma taf'alu min khayrin fa-innallaha kana bihi 'alima'.

Brevity in quoting the Question is Eloquence: In 'wa yastaftunaka fin-nisa'' (And they request from you a ruling concerning women), there is a summary similar to the summary in 'and they ask you about the sacred months.' We explained there that questions from people are generally described with extreme summary in the Quran, and this method is in accord with eloquence. When the nature of the question becomes clear from the answer itself, what need remains for lengthiness in quoting the question?

"Al-Kitab" here refers to verses 3 and 4 of this Surah: 'wa ma yutla 'alaykum fil-kitab' is conjoined to the genitive pronoun in 'fihinna', and 'Al-Kitab' refers to the Quran, and the hint here is toward verses 3 and 4 of this very Surah, regarding whose described command the questions are being answered here. 'Yutla' (is recited) is in the present tense for painting the picture of the situation, because at that time these verses were under teaching and also under discussion in every circle. 

Clarification of the conditions of paying Mahr and Justice in Marriage: 'fi yataman-nisa'il-lati la tu'tunahunna ma kutiba lahunna wa targhabuna an tankihuhunna wal-mustadh'afina minal-wildan' is a summary reference toward the command described in those verses. Meaning: this fatwa is also regarding the command given to you concerning the orphan children of those women. Those women to whom you are not ready to give their right yet are desirous of marrying them. From this, incidentally, the point also emerged that in 'if you fear that you will not justly deal with the orphans, then marry those that please you of women and give the women their bridal gifts', "women" refers to the mothers of the orphans, as we have adopted; and the hint also emerged that people wanted to marry them for the benefit of the orphans but the condition of Mahr and justice was heavy upon them. 'ma kutiba lahunna' means what has been appointed for them by God. Obviously, just as the condition of Mahr was made necessary in their case, the condition of justice was also made. For this reason, both these things will enter into its meaning.

Answer to the Question and a Style of the Arabic Language: 'wa an taqumu lil-yatama bil-qist' (and that you stand for the orphans in justice) is that fatwa which was revealed in response to the request for a ruling. But here, this style of the Arabic language should be remembered: when a conjunction appears such that its 'ma'tuf 'alayh' (conjoined part) is not present in the speech, there is room to assume those points as omitted 'ma'tuf 'alayh' which are evidenced by context. More than one example of this has passed before, and extremely clear examples of this will come ahead in this book. Here, no such word is present in the speech that can become the 'ma'tuf 'alayh' for 'wa an taqumu'. For this reason, an omission must necessarily be assumed here, and this omission will be determined in the light of the context. Consequently, here before 'wa an taqumu', this subject will be omitted: give those women their Mahr, deal with them with justice; then the conjunction of 'wa an taqumu lil-yatama bil-qist' will be suitable upon it—meaning: and become those who protect justice for the orphans. Effectively, it was clarified in the fatwa that the condition of Mahr and justice exists regarding the mothers of orphans just as it exists regarding general women, and the command for justice with women in 'if you fear that you will not justly deal...' and the command for paying Mahr in 'and give the women their bridal gifts...' relates to the mothers of orphans, whom you want to marry but are not ready to get into the 'trouble' (karkhera) of Mahr and justice. In this way, the Quran effectively opened the summary of verses 3-4 and further emphasized the commands given in them through this fatwa.

Meaning of Verse 127 in light of the Composition of Speech: Since there is great difference in the interpretation of this verse, and this difference is mostly the result of not understanding the composition of the speech, therefore, reflect on the composition of the speech in light of our explanation and embed it well in the mind. In this light, the meaning of the verses will be: people are asking you questions regarding the Mahr of women and justice between different wives, specifically regarding the Mahr of the mothers of orphans and justice between them—that when the original benefit in marrying them is for their own children, will the condition of Mahr and justice be mandatory in their case too just as it is regarding other women? He stated: tell them that Allah gives a fatwa regarding general women and also regarding those mothers of orphans whose ruling was described in verses 3-4—whom you want to marry but do not want to acknowledge the right of justice and Mahr for them—and also regarding helpless orphans: that you give them their Mahr, deal with them with justice, and become establishers of truth and justice for the orphans. Furthermore, whatever goodness and kind treatment you perform, Allah will be aware of it and you will find its reward with God.

'wa in-imra'atun khafat mim-ba'liha nushuzan aw i'radhan fa-la junaha 'alayhima ay-yushliha baynahuma shulha wash-shulhu khayr wa uhdhiratil-anfusu-sh-shuhh wa in tuhsinu wa tattaqu fa-innallaha kana bi-ma ta'maluna khabira'.

Discussion on the word "Nushuz" passed previously. If 'Nushuz' is from the woman's side, its meaning is that she does not acknowledge the man's 'Qawwam-ship.' If it is from the man's side, its meaning is that he refuses to acknowledge the wife's rights and becomes ready to get rid of her. 

'wa uhdhiratil-anfusu-sh-shuhh': 'shuhh' means both miserliness and greed. 'Miserliness' is for a person to be narrow-hearted in fulfilling rights; this thing is reprehensible in every state. But 'greed' can be for a good thing too, and for a bad thing too; it can be within limits and outside limits; for this reason, its being good or bad is a relative matter. In terms of its good aspect, it holds its own place within human nature, but such a dominance of it occurs over most temperaments that it remains a disease. In 'wa uhdhiratil-anfusu-sh-shuhh', its this very aspect is expressed.

Urging both Woman and Man toward Sacrifice to maintain the Bond of Marriage: Meaning: Mahr and justice are a Shari right of every woman, but if any woman fears from her husband that if she kept these burdens of restrictions loaded upon him, he would leave her or treat her with indifference, then there is no harm in the two together making some compromise. Meaning: the woman gives such concessions to the husband regarding her right of Mahr, justice, and maintenance that the apprehension of severing ties is removed. It stated: goodness lies in peace and compromise, because once the bond of husband and wife is established, the success of the parties lies in it remaining established, even if much sacrifice has to be made for it. It stated: greed is a general disease of temperaments that affects mutual relationships, and its remedy is either for both parties to be ready for sacrifice, or if one party's disease is incurable, for the other to be ready for sacrifice. In short, to maintain the bond of marriage, even if the woman has to give a sacrifice, goodness lies in its remaining established. After that, through the words 'wa in tuhsinu wa tattaqu...', the man was urged: the field of sacrifice, selflessness, excellence, and Taqwa is originally befitting of him; he should respect his chivalry and manhood and become a giver to the woman instead of a taker from her. Allah is aware of every deed of everyone and He will give a full reward for every good deed.

129-130: 'wa lan tastati'u an ta'dilu baynan-nisa'i wa law harashtum fa-la tamilu kullal-mayli fa-tadharuha kal-mu'allaqah wa in tushlihu wa tattaqu fa-innallaha kana ghafurar-rahima. wa iy-yatafarraqa yughnillahu kullam-min sa'atih wa kanallahu wasi'an hakima'.

The Standard of Justice regarding Wives: Now the meaning of justice was clarified: the justice you are calling impossible is your own mental justice. You are thinking that heart-felt attachment and outward treatment are both required to be exactly equal. For this reason, this point appears impossible. There is no doubt that in this sense, respecting justice is beyond your power. Even if you wanted to perform this type of justice, you could not. The heart's inclination is not a thing in a person's own choice. The thing required is: do not lean toward one wife in such a way that the other is left hanging in the middle, but try to maintain a balance in treatment and rights, and if some deprivation of rights or shortcoming occurs, try to reform and make amends for it and remain fearful of God. Despite this effort for reform and Taqwa, if some oversight occurred, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.

The Limit of Sacrifice: Following this, it stated: what is required in the Sharia is indeed that the marital bond does not break, but if circumstances do force it and separation occurs between the two, then nonetheless the original Provider and Designer of both the man and the woman is Allah. He will make each of them independent through His favor; He is All-Encompassing and Wise. The meaning is: to maintain this relationship, sacrifice and effort are required from both husband and wife, but this is required alongside the protection of honor and self-respect. Just as it is not permissible for either the husband or the wife to be stiff-necked, it is also not permissible to be suppressed more than a specific limit. Although there is generality in the words, the context proves that in this is a specific encouragement for women: that they should try to maintain as much as possible and even sacrifice for reconciliation, but they should hold the courage that if despite the effort the form of maintenance was not produced, the Provider is Allah Almighty. He will make them independent from His treasury of generosity.

131-134: 'wa lillahi ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ardh wa laqad wash-shaynal-ladhina utul-kitaba min qablikum wa iyyakum an-ittaqu-llah wa in takfuru fa-inna lillahi ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ardh wa kanallahu ghaniyyan hamida. wa lillahi ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ardh wa kafa billahi wakila. iy-yasha' yudh-hibkum ayyu-han-nasu wa ya'ti bi-akharin wa kanallahu 'ala dhalika qadira. man kana yuridu thawab-ad-dunya fa-'indallahi thawab-ad-dunya wal-akhirah wa kanallahu sami'am-bashira'.

Two points are particularly worth mentioning in these verses:

Eloquence of the Repetition of 'wa lillahi ma fis-samawati...': First, that "And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth" is repeated several times. Twice in verse 131 itself and then in verse 132. Furthermore, where this subject starts, i.e., verse 126, the exact same segment is there. The repeated recurrence of this subject is not without cause but was necessitated by the temperament of the Surah's conclusion. We hinted above that in this conclusion, a final warning was given to the Muslims, the hypocrites, and the People of the Book: the instructions necessary for your guidance were given with the clarification of arguments; now believing or not believing is your job; if you believe, it is your benefit; if you do not believe, you will not harm God at all; God is the Master of this entire universe without any partner; His government is established on its own strength; He is not in need of anyone. Since He is the Master of the entire universe, He informed the People of the Book before you of His commands and limits, and now He has informed you of them too: remain fearful of God. The People of the Book disobeyed, so they invited their own wretchedness. They did not harm God. Similarly, if you too commit Kufr, remember that God is not in need of you but is Independent and Praiseworthy. If He likes something for you, it is not because He has a need for it but because He is 'Hamid' (Praiseworthy). The demand of this attribute of His is that despite being independent, He blesses the entire creation with His generosity. Due to being the Master of the entire universe, He alone is deserving that trust be placed in Him and the reins of life be handed to Him. In the case of disobedience, if He wills, He can erase all of you and settle other creatures in this world. He is powerful over everything. Who can catch His hand?

A Style of Omission: The second noteworthy thing is the style of omission in the verse 'Whoever desires the reward of this world...'. We hinted elsewhere that in Arabic, out of two contrasting components of speech, some components are omitted such that the mentioned component points toward the omitted one itself. In our view, if the omissions of this verse were opened, the composition of the speech would be: 'man kana yuridu thawab-ad-dunya fa-'indallahi thawab-ad-dunya wa man kana yuridu thawab-al-akhirati fa-'indallahi thawab-al-akhirah'. In the first, 'fa-'indallahi thawab-ad-dunya' was omitted, and from the second, 'wa man kana yuridu thawab-al-akhirati'. The reason for this omission is the same one to which we hinted: the mentioned segments are themselves pointing out the omitted segments. The meaning is: for the one who is a seeker of only the world's reward—the Master of the world too is God; He gives them from it as much as He wills; and for the one who is a seeker of the Hereafter—Allah gives them in the world too what He wills and will also bestow the reward of the Hereafter in full. This is a warning and admonition to those who are fleeing from God's Sharia for the sake of their worldly interests. It stated: the one who becomes a seeker of only the world—nonetheless, they obtain from it only as much as is approved by God and remain entirely deprived of the Hereafter; so why not become a seeker of the Hereafter so that the full reward of the Hereafter is obtained and what is destined from the world is also obtained? This exact same subject has already passed in Surah Al Imran verses 145 and 148. Cast a glance at that too. Alongside this, the purpose of referencing the attributes of 'Sami'' and 'Basir' is to remind of the reality: God is neither unaware of anyone's prayer and plea, nor is anyone's need and state hidden from Him; so why after all should a human not seek and ask from Him alone? Why should they be hopeful and a seeker of justice from others?

Subject of Verses 135 to 152: Further on, first the Muslims were reminded of the official duty for which Allah Almighty commissioned them after dismissing the People of the Book; then He also alerted them toward the dangers of the hypocrites and warned the hypocrites. The structure of the discourse is perfectly clear. Recite the following verses:

'ya-ayyu-hal-ladhina amanu kunu qawwamina bil-qisti shuhada'a lillahi wa law 'ala anfusikum awil-walidayni wal-aqrabina iy-yakun ghaniyyan aw faqiran fallahu awla bihima fa-la tattabi'ul-hawa an ta'dilu wa in talwu aw tu'ridhu fa-innallaha kana bi-ma ta'maluna khabira'.

'kunu qawwamina bil-qisti shuhada'a lillahi'. Discussion on the word "Qist" passed under Al Imran verses 18 and 21. Here it refers to that balance of truth and justice which Allah Almighty granted in the form of His Book. The meaning is: now your every word and deed must be weighed by this weight and this scale, and you must become those who remain firm upon it and those who establish it. 'Shuhada'a lillah' (Witnesses for Allah)—meaning merely remaining firm upon it is not your only responsibility, but you are also the callers and witnesses on behalf of Allah before the world, as stated: 'And thus We have made you a middle nation that you will be witnesses over the people and the Messenger will be a witness over you.'

Remain firm on Justice whether in your favor or against you: 'wa law 'ala anfusikum' (even if it be against yourselves)—meaning this balance of justice is not only for taking but also for giving. If its decision in some matter is against you, your parents, or your relatives, you must nonetheless remain firm upon it and bear witness to it. This also contains a hint toward the fact that the Jews were given the Book, but they followed only those points they found in accord with their desires, while they avoided the points that went against their desires. 

A Single Weight and a Single Scale for both Wealthy and Poor: 'iy-yakun ghaniyyan aw faqiran fallahu awla bihima'. Meaning: it is not permissible to weigh with one weight for the wealthy and another for the poor, for there to be a separate law and Sharia for the powerful and influential and a separate command and fatwa for the weak and uninfluential; rather, everyone must be under the single law of justice of Allah, because Allah's right is established equally over all, and this right is greater than all other rights. If someone is wealthy and influential, they are not absolved of God's right such that they be exempt from the responsibilities of God's law and be dealt with under some other law. To understand this point well, keep in sight this statement of the Prophet reported by Lady Aisha (RA). She says: "When a Makhzumi woman committed theft, the Quraysh became very concerned about her case. People began thinking: who could be the one to intercede for her with the Messenger of Allah (PBUH)? Finally it was settled that only Usama bin Zaid could dare to do so; he is very beloved to the Messenger of Allah (PBUH). Upon people's request, Usama interceded for her with the Prophet. The Prophet said: 'Usama, have you come to intercede regarding one of the limits [hudud] appointed by Allah?' Then you stood for a sermon and stated: 'O people! The thing that destroyed the nations before you was that their state became such that if a noble person among them stole, they would leave him; and if an ordinary person stole, they would implement the limit upon him. By God! I am not one to do so. I, even if Fatima daughter of Muhammad had stolen, would have cut off her hand too.'" (Agreed upon).

"Following Desires" (ittiba' al-hawa) is the opposite of "Huda Allah" (Allah's Guidance): 'fa-la tattabi'ul-hawa an ta'dilu'. 'Hawa' is the opposite of 'Huda Allah'—meaning if you abandon this guidance of Allah to follow your desires and innovations, you will deviate from that 'Qist' (equity) upon which Allah Almighty established you and to whose call and testimony you were commissioned.

Two forms of Corrupting the System of Equity: 'wa in talwu aw tu'ridhu'. In this, an indication was made toward two forms of corrupting this system of equity. One is to try to twist, corrupt, and distort it, as the Jews did and as mentioned in Al Imran verse 78: 'they twist their tongues with the Book...'. The second form is that the attempt to corrupt it is not made, its form remains, but no importance is given to it in the transactions of life. It stated: whichever of these injustices you commit, God will not remain unaware of it; and when He does not remain unaware, the mandatory result is that He will not leave this great crime without punishment.
