'ya-ayyu-hal-ladhina amanu aminu billahi wa rasulihi wal-kitab-illadhi nazzala 'ala rasulihi wal-kitab-illadhi anzala min qabl wa may-yakfur billahi wa mala'ikatihi wa kutubihi wa rusulihi wal-yawmil-akhiri faqad dhalla dhalalam-ba'ida'.

A Style of Language: 'ya-ayyu-hal-ladhina amanu aminu' (O you who have believed, believe). We clarified this style elsewhere: a verb is used for its initial and outward meaning and also for its true and perfect meaning. Here the Muslims as a collective are addressed, among whom were included all types of elements—raw and mature, defective and perfect, and sincere and hypocrite. Addressing all of them, the warning was given: O you who claim faith! Become true and firm believers. Effectively, the address is general, but the focus of the speech is toward the raw ones and the mere claimants.

Before the Quran, the status of the Original Divine Book was held only by the Torah: 'wal-kitab-illadhi anzala min qabl' (and the Book He revealed before)—in this, 'Book' refers to the Torah. The reason for mentioning it in the singular is that before the Quran, the status of the original Divine Book was in reality held only by the Torah; the status of the scriptures of other prophets is not that of independent, self-subsisting scriptures because all those prophets came as callers to the Torah alone. So much so that even Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) came in reality to establish the Torah. The teaching in those prophets' scriptures is not something separate from the Torah but is the call to its revival and renewal and the expression of its commands and secrets. For this reason, in terms of reality, it is a single Book; but if the outward is considered, it can also be designated as plural. The Quran has mentioned it in both ways, and the objective is to draw attention to the original reality so that those ignorant ones who made the outward plurality a means for 'discrimination between the Messengers' (tafriq bayn al-rusul) are warned of their folly.

Difference between "Anzala" and "Nazzala": The difference between "Nazzala" and "Anzala" is also worth noting here. Those acquainted with the nuances of the Arabic language know that the meaning of "Anzala" is merely to send down, but within "Nazzala" is found the meaning of special care and graduality. This difference in words clarifies the nature of the sending down of both the Torah and the Quran. A hint here is sufficient. We will discuss this with clarification in some suitable place.

This Verse is the Middle Link between the Previous and the Following: All the components of faith mentioned in this verse have been discussed in detail in Surah Al-Baqarah. The point to understand here is that this verse holds the status of the middle link between the previous verse and the following verses. On one hand, it points toward that comprehensive declaration (Kalimah Jami'ah) which is the declaration of the 'Ummat-e-Wasat' and the 'Ummah standing firmly for justice'—meaning faith in all prophets and messengers and all heavenly scriptures without distinction, which is testimony to the fact that this Ummah is established on justice and equity; unlike the Jews and Christians, it has not abandoned the highway of truth and justice by becoming afflicted with the madness of prejudice and sectarianism. On the other hand, it is the preamble to the mention of those hypocrites who were either themselves from the Jews or were secretly under their influence. For this reason, they were afflicted with exactly the same misguidances that were the Jews' heritage; consequently, the entire reality was disclosed by the subsequent verses.

137-138: 'innal-ladhina amanu thumma kafaru thumma amanu thumma kafaru thummaz-dadu kufral-lam yakunillahu liyagh-fira lahum wa la liyah-diyahum sabila. bash-shiril-munafiqina bi-anna lahum 'adhaban alima'.

The Character of the Hypocrites who came from within the People of the Book: After clarifying the correct stance and station of the believers, attention was turned toward the hypocrites: those who believed then disbelieved, then believed then disbelieved, then increased in their disbelief—they are not worthy of God's forgiveness and guidance; deliver the 'glad tidings' of God's painful punishment to these hypocrites. The point that the mention here is of the hypocrites is clear from these very verses of the Quran. Through the words "bash-shir al-munafiqin" (give glad tidings to the hypocrites), it was clarified whose character has been described. However, the question worth considering is: this state of 'faith then disbelief, then faith then disbelief' described for them—is it merely a picture of their wavering or a statement of fact? Our thought is that it is a statement of fact. The reason, as we hinted above, is that these hypocrites were mostly from the People of the Book—especially the Jews—and were also under their influence. From this perspective, one game of their faith and disbelief is the one they already played with the Torah, and the second game is this one they are playing with Islam: that first they stepped forward to acknowledge believing in it, and now day and night they are in pursuit of plotting against it. It stated: now God is neither going to forgive them nor show them any other path. 'Showing any other path' means that now the proof has been completed over them; now no further trial for them is needed. Now only the path of Hell remains for them. Further on, this subject was clarified thus: 'Indeed, those who disbelieve and commit wrong - never will Allah forgive them, nor will He guide them to a path. Except the path of Hell, they will abide therein forever...' This same subject was expressed here as 'bash-shir al-munafiqin bi-anna lahum 'adhaban alima'.

139-140: 'alladhina yattakhidhunal-kafirina awliya'a min dunil-mu'minina a-yabtaghuna 'indahumul-'izzata fa-innal-'izzata lillahi jami'a. wa qad nazzala 'alaykum fil-kitabi an idha sami'tum ayatillahi yukfaru biha wa yustahza'u biha fa-la taq'udu ma'ahum hatta yakhudhu fi hadithin ghayrihi innakum idham-mithluhum innallaha jami'ul-munafiqina wal-kafirina fi jahannama jami'a'.

"wa qad nazzala 'alaykum fil-kitab" refers to Surah Al-An'am verse 68: In "wa qad nazzala 'alaykum fil-kitab", the reference is to verse 68 of Surah Al-An'am. There it stated: 'And when you see those who engage in [offensive] discourse concerning Our verses, then turn away from them until they enter into another conversion. And if Satan should cause you to forget, then do not remain after the reminder with the wrongdoing people.' Then this subject was also described in verse 70 of that same Al-An'am. 

Mocking Allah's Verses in Assemblies: 'alladhina yattakhidhuna...', this describes the attribute of the hypocrites: that they have made the disbelievers—i.e., the Jews—their friends and designers instead of the believers. They are desirous of obtaining honor and success in their eyes, even though honor and disgrace are all in God's authority. He gives honor to whom He wills and disgraces whom He wills. These people attend their assemblies where Allah's verses are mocked. Even though this clear instruction has been revealed in the Quran: when you see that Allah's verses are being denied and mocked, do not sit with such people until these mockers engage in some other talk. If someone does so, they too become their companion; therefore, Allah will gather such hypocrites with those disbelievers in Hell. In assemblies where God's religion and His Sharia are desecrated, if a Muslim participates, it is proof of their lack of pride and lack of honor. If someone considers participation in them an honor for themselves, this is proof not only of a lack of pride but also of being divested of faith. The resurrection of this type of hypocrite will be with those people with whom they were partners in mocking God's religion. From this verse, some important principles of calling to the religion also emerge, but discussion on them will come in the suitable place in Surah Al-An'am.

'alladhina yatarabbashuna bikum fa-in kana lakum fathum-minallahi qalu a-lam nakum-ma'akum wa in kana lil-kafirina nashibun qalu a-lam nastahwidh 'alaykum wa namna'kum-minal-mu'minina fallahu yahkumu baynakum yawmal-qiyamati wa lay-yaj'alallahu lil-kafirina 'alal-mu'minina sabila'.

Meaning of 'istahwadha 'alayh': 'istahwadha 'alayh' means 'ahata bihi', 'ghalaba', 'istawla 'alayh'—he encompassed him, he prevailed over him, he took him into his grip. When a male takes a female into his grip and does not allow any other male to approach her, this word is also used for that. 

'yatarabbashuna bikum' means 'yatarabbashuna bikum-ad-dawa'ir'—they remain waiting for turns of fortune against you. 

Two-sided Machinations of the Hypocrites: This is further detail of these hypocrites' character: they are extremely ill-wishing toward Islam and the Muslims. They are always in wait for some fall to face you, some stumble to occur, or for you to suffer defeat. If you achieve victory, they will say: "Were we not with you?" And if the enemy's side happens to be heavier somewhere, they will reach them and assure them: "This was our strategy that you remained safe from the Muslims. we kept you protected in this way such that the Muslims could not attack you openly and their full pressure could not fall upon you." It stated: today they are managing with these word-makings, but a day will come when all circumstances will be unveiled; on that Day, God will decide between you and them, and nothing of theirs will prevail against you. On that Day, they will not be able to make the claim of "Were we not with you?"

142-143: 'innal-munafiqina yukhadhi'unallaha wa huwa khadi'uhum wa idha qamu ilash-shalati qamu kusala yura'unan-nasa wa la yadhkurunallaha illa qalila. mudhabdhabina bayna dhalika la ila ha'ula'i wa la ila ha'ula'i wa may-yudhlilillahu fa-lan tajida lahu sabila'.

'yukhadhi'unallaha wa huwa khadi'uhum': detailed discussion on every aspect of this passed under verse 9 of Surah Al-Baqarah. 

Picture of the Hypocrites' Wavering: 'mudhabdhabina bayna dhalik'. 'dhabdhaba-sh-shay'' means the thing is moving while hanging in the air. 'dhabdhaba-r-rajulu' means the person is confused and hesitant. 'bayna dhalika la ila ha'ula'i wa la ila ha'ula'i'—meaning neither with the Muslims nor with the disbelievers, confused and helpless between the two; sometimes going to the Muslims to assure them "we are with you," sometimes reaching the disbelievers to assure them "we are with you," even though they are with no one; like a goat wandering between two flocks, sometimes joining this flock and sometimes the other. Note that 'kusala' (lazy), 'yura'una' (showing off), and 'mudhabdhabin' (wavering) are all in the state of condition (Hal). Bring all three into the eye of imagination simultaneously, then the correct picture will come forward. 

The one attempting to Deceive Allah is Deceived themselves: The meaning is: these hypocrites are not only deceiving Allah's servants but want to deceive God too, even though the one who wants to deceive God does not deceive God but deceives only themselves; this is because God lengthens their rope, from which they think they have deceived God, whereas they were deceived by God. 'wa idha qamu ilash-shalati...' is an example of this deception of theirs: meaning, they rise for prayer by forcing their nature, lazily, and under compulsion, merely out of the fear that if they do not participate in the congregation, their name will be removed from the register of Muslims. This is merely a prayer for show so that Muslims consider them included among themselves; for this reason, the remembrance of Allah in it is only as much as can be in a forced prayer and a prayer for show. Obviously, this is explicit deception with Allah. It stated: these are outcasts from God; He has left them to wander, and for those whom God has left to wander, who now can bring them to the path? 

In the First Century, Attendance at the Mosque was the Distinguishing Mark between Faith and Disbelief: From this verse, the point is clearly manifest that in that era, attendance at the mosque held the status of a distinguishing mark between faith and disbelief. The person who remained absent from the mosque without any known excuse—it became impossible for them to have themselves counted among the Muslims. There was that era, or now this era has come: let alone attendance at the congregation, even performing prayer at all is not necessary for being considered a Muslim—in fact, for being accepted as a leader of Muslims. How strange!

'ya-ayyu-hal-ladhina amanu la tattakhidhul-kafirina awliya'a min dunil-mu'minina a-turiduna an taj'alu lillahi 'alaykum sultanam-mubina'.

"Al-Kafirin" although general, the context proves that the People of the Book are intended by it, with whom the hypocrites had machinations. The qualifier "min dunil-mu'minin" (instead of the believers) expresses the point that making disbelievers friends and allies is prohibited only when it is in opposition to the Muslims. If this form is not present, there is no evil in it.

"sultanam-mubina" (a clear authority/proof): 

Friendship with Disbelievers in opposition to Muslims is Proof of Disbelief: Although the address is general, the focus of the speech is toward the hypocrites: do not make the disbelievers your friends and companions in opposition to the Muslims. This crime is no ordinary crime. By committing this crime, you will give Allah such a decisive proof against yourselves that no room for any excuse will remain for you. Your Kufr will become perfectly certain and your being deserving of Hell perfectly proven.

145-147: 'innal-munafiqina fid-darkil-asfali minan-nari wa lan tajida lahum nashira. illal-ladhina tabu wa aslahu wa'tashamu billahi wa akhlashu dinahum lillahi fa-ula'ika ma'al-mu'minina wa sawfa yu'tilla-hul-mu'minina ajran 'azhima. ma yaf'alullahu bi-'adhabikum in shakartum wa amantum wa kanallahu shakiran 'alima'.

Meaning of 'Ad-Dark al-Asfal': 'Ad-Dark al-Asfal'—'dark' means the furthest bottom of a thing; thus, the lowest part of something.

'Din' in the meaning of Obedience: 'wa akhlashu dinahum': we have discussed the various meanings of the word 'Din' elsewhere. Among others, its meaning is also obedience. For example: 'Say, "Indeed, I have been commanded to worship Allah, [being] sincere to Him in religion [obedience]."'

The Rank of the Hypocrites is even lower than the Disbelievers: This is the final warning to the hypocrites: let no one remain in the misconception that in comparison to explicit Kufr, this wavering faith of theirs nonetheless holds some value. It stated: no, these hypocrites will be in the lowest level of Hell—meaning their rank is even lower than the rank of the staunch and hostile disbelievers. Nothing except repentance can save them from this end. And for this repentance, the conditions of reform, holding fast to Allah, and sincerity are placed. Reform—meaning they reform their conduct; holding fast—meaning they grasp Allah's rope firmly; sincerity—meaning they perform sincere obedience to Allah and the Messenger, without any wavering or show. It stated: then they can be with the Muslims in the Hereafter. And they should be assured that with Allah, there is a great reward for the believers. After that, addressing these hypocrites themselves, He stated in the style of digression: God has no benefit in punishing you. If you become grateful and adopt faith, then Allah is very Appreciative and well-aware of everyone's faith and action. 

Note that in terms of the philosophy of religion, gratitude (shukr) is the fountainhead of true faith. Furthermore, note that sometimes the meaning of words changes with a change in relationship. Consequently, when 'shukr' is related to God, its meaning becomes 'acceptance/appreciation.' Discussion on both these points passed elsewhere.

148-149: 'la yuhibbullahu-l-jahra bis-su'i minal-qawli illa man zhulima wa kanallahu sami'an 'alima. in tubdu khayran aw tukhfuhu aw ta'fu 'an su'in fa-innallaha kana 'afuwwan qadira'.

Expression of Evil with the determination of Persons is permissible only for the Oppressed: This is a warning to the Muslims similar to the warning that passed in verse 86: just as there, when the command to turn away from the hypocrites occurred, the Muslims were simultaneously instructed that whoever greets you, you respond to their greeting—and the objective was that the passionate Muslims do not end all talk and greetings with those whom they suspect of hypocrisy. Similarly here, since the words up to 'in the lowest depths of the Fire' have been used for the hypocrites in the previous verses—which carried the apprehension that Muslims would begin the open expression and announcement of the hypocrites' evils in harsh words—therefore this instruction was given: the expression of evil with the determination of specific persons is permissible only for the one who has been oppressed. For others, Allah does not like it.

An Important Instruction for Communal Life: Since this is among the extremely important issues of communal life, it should be well-understood. In communal life, if some evil is taking root or has taken root within a group which could become a threat to the entire community, then its redress is necessary; and for this redress, it is also necessary that the repulsiveness and loathsomeness of this evil, its bad results, and the end of its perpetrators be well-clarified so that the members of the community remain protected from its evil. But simultaneously, it is also necessary to respect the matter that the general members of the community do not begin to apply a point said in a general form to specific persons merely on the basis of their own estimate, analogy, and assumption. From this, there is not only the apprehension that many innocent persons will become targets of slanders, but also the danger of disorder and corruption being produced in the community. The points described here regarding the hypocrites—see, they are described in a perfectly general form, and the objective is that those who are committing these acts reform themselves if they wish, and if they do not reform, at least the Muslims keep themselves protected from these Fitnas. To this extent, this thing is not only correct but indispensable for communal survival. But if this same thing takes the form that general individuals make it an argument to specifically target each other with insults—"you are a hypocrite," "you have become a disbeliever," and "so-and-so is deserving of the lowest depths of the Fire"—then a storm will be triggered in the entire community. To block this Fitna, this instruction was given: the expression and announcement of evil with the determination of persons is permissible only for that person who has been personally oppressed. In this form, the oppression, the oppressor, and the oppressed will all be determined, and the law will be able to provide the remedy. Until this form exists, the point should be said in a general form alone, just as the Quran has said. When such an evil came to the Holy Prophet's (PBUH) knowledge too, he always rebuked people for it in a general form alone. His general style of speech would be: "What has happened to the people who do such-and-such?" However, when a determined person came forward with a determined crime, he would seize upon them according to the law. 

The purpose of mentioning Divine Attributes is their mandatory result: 'wa kanallahu sami'an 'alima' is as a warning: meaning if any person adopts a way contrary to this instruction, they should remember that God is All-Hearing and All-Knowing. We have stated this principle in more than one place: when Divine attributes are referenced like this, the objective is their mandatory result—meaning when God hears and knows everything, He will also necessarily seize upon it. 

Statement of the Preferred Path: 'in tubdu khayran...' (If you reveal good...). After describing the path Allah dislikes, this is the statement of the preferred path. It stated: the preferred path is for a person to express a good point, nurture a good passion in the heart, and overlook the evils of others. After that, He referenced His two attributes: 'Afuww' (Pardoning) and 'Qadir' (Powerful), from which the hint emerges toward the reality that God overlooks the evils of people despite possessing all kinds of power. For this reason, He wants a reflection of these attributes of His to be found within His servants too. A person possesses the power to respond to someone in kind, but despite this, they overlook—this is 'Afw' (pardon).

150-152: 'innal-ladhina yakfuruna billahi wa rusulihi wa yuriduna ay-yufarriqu baynallahi wa rusulihi wa yaquluna nu'minu bi-ba'dhiw-wa nakfuru bi-ba'dhiw-wa yuriduna ay-yattakhidhu bayna dhalika sabila. ula'ika humul-kafiruna haqqa wa a'tadna lil-kafirina 'adhabam-muhina. wal-ladhina amanu billahi wa rusulihi wa lam yufarriqu bayna ahadim-minhum ula'ika sawfa yu'tihim ujurahum wa kanallahu ghafurar-rahima'.

The People of the Book are Staunch Disbelievers: The exegesis of every component of the 'indictment' of the People of the Book described in these verses has passed in previous Surahs. However, their occasion and place require clarification. It passed under verse 144 above that in 'do not take the disbelievers as allies,' 'disbelievers' refers to the People of the Book. Although the People of the Book's Kufr is perfectly clear, the pretext-seeking natures—who wanted to maintain machinations with them—were not ready to cut their links with them; they carved this Shari pretext to justify their attitude: that the People of the Book are nonetheless People of the Book; some corruptions can and do exist within them from the viewpoint of religion, but on the basis of these corruptions, it is not in accord with justice to stand them entirely in the rank of disbelievers and deal with them the same way. The Quran unveiled the deception of these pretext-seekers in these verses and stated with extreme clarity: the staunch disbelievers are in reality these People of the Book, because they discriminate between Allah and His messengers; they believe in whichever of those God sent as His messengers they wish, and they do not believe in whichever they wish. The meaning is: they want to bring faith in God on their own terms, not on God's terms, even though faith is authoritative only when it is on God's terms. If they are the ones to appoint the conditions of faith and they perform the selection of messengers on their own discretion, then where did God's divinity remain? Then they themselves have assumed God's position. It stated: there is not a bit of doubt in their being staunch disbelievers, and for such self-deluded and arrogant disbelievers, We have prepared a humiliating punishment. Believers will only be counted as those who believed in Allah and all His messengers, making no distinction among them. These people will undoubtedly receive their reward. God is Forgiving and Merciful. 

From this verse, the reality was clarified: Kufr is not only for a person to explicitly deny God and the messengers, but it is also Kufr—and explicit Kufr—that they believe in God and His messengers but on their own conditions.

Subject of Verses 153 to 162: Further on, there is a warning to the People of the Book—the Jews and Christians—and this warning is so hard and severe that the surge of wrath is overflowing from every word. The entire speech from beginning to end consists only of the indictment of crimes, and the surge and flow of the discourse is such that after the point starts, it is difficult to determine where it ended. In this type of passionate and wrathful speech, the predicate (khabar) is generally omitted; effectively, the speaker's passion itself becomes the substitute for the predicate, and the objective is understood from the subject (mubtada') alone. Recite the verses.

Exegesis of Verses 153 to 155: 'yas'aluka ahlul-kitabi an tunazzila 'alayhim kitabam-minas-sama'i faqad sa'alu Musa akbara min dhalika fa-qalu arinallaha jahratan fa-akhadhathumus-sha'iqatu bi-zhulmihim thummattakhadhul-'ijla mim-ba'di ma ja'at-humul-bayyinatu fa-'afawna 'an dhalika wa atayna Musa sultanam-mubina. wa rafa'na fawqahumut-tura bi-mithaqihim wa qulna lahum-udkhulul-baba sujjadaw-wa qulna lahum la ta'du fis-sabti wa akhadhna minhum mithaqan ghalizha. fa-bi-ma naqdhihim mithaqahum wa kufrihim bi-ayatillahi wa qatlahumul-anbiya'a bi-ghayri haqqiw-wa qawlihim qulubuna ghulfum-bal taba'allahu 'alayha bi-kufrihim fa-la yu'minuna illa qalila'.

The events of the history of the Children of Israel referenced in these verses have all without exception been discussed in the exegesis of Surah Al-Baqarah. See the exegesis of verses 47 to 96. 

Meaning of "bi-zhulmihim": 'bi-zhulmihim' (due to their wrongdoing)—meaning they invited this thunderbolt through their own wretchedness. Allah did not commit injustice against them; rather, they wronged their own souls. They insisted on an experience they could not endure; the result was that they fell under its strike. 

Meaning of "Sultanum-Mubin": 'Sultanum-Mubin' refers to that decisive proof which Allah Almighty granted to Sayyidina Moses (PBUH) in the form of miracles. These miracles were so silencing and overriding that after them, no room for hesitation remained for any lover of justice. 

Raising the Mount (Tur) and the Covenant: 'wa rafa'na fawqahumut-tura bi-mithaqihim'. In our view, the 'Ba' is for the meaning of 'attachment.' Under Al-Baqarah verse 63, we clarified the reality of raising the Mount and described its purpose: the objective of manifesting this overriding power was to clarify to the Children of Israel that the God with whom they are making this treaty—this mountain-shaking power is in His hand. If the treaty was broken after being made, remember that no one will be able to save you from the punishment of that covenant-breaking. Here, this reality was pictured thus: God raised Mount Tur over them and also the treaty with it—that this is the treaty and this is the mountain; if this treaty is desecrated, your head will be crushed with this very stone.

'bal taba'allahu 'alayha' (rather, Allah has set a seal upon them) holds the status of an incidental phrase. The meaning of their statement "our hearts are wrapped" and the eloquence of this incidental phrase and its reality were described in Surah Al-Baqarah. 

A Nuance of Eloquence: In this entire section, this nuance of eloquence is worth noting: a long list of the Children of Israel's crimes was recited, but the objective of reciting this list was not clarified in the words. In the middle of the list of crimes, an incidental phrase appeared, and as soon as it ended, the series of describing their crimes began again. After that, due to the requirement of the speech, another long incidental phrase appeared, and as soon as it closed, the list of crimes began again. This style of expression, as we hinted in the introduction, expresses the speaker's force of expression and passion, the listener's intelligence and alertness, the power and clarity of the claim, and the decision being independent of explicit statement. In the sermons of Arab orators, extremely fine examples of this are found. Extremely eloquent examples of this will also come ahead in the Quran. A listener with taste can understand this type of forceful speech, but it is not possible to transfer its force and its eloquence into another language.

The Objective of the Speech in our own words: The meaning is: the Jews are demanding of you that they will believe in the Quran and your Messengership only when you send down a Book upon them from heaven such that they see it descending with their own eyes. Do not wonder at this demand of theirs. Those whose predecessors they are the successors of—they have already made a demand far greater than this of their Prophet. These only want to see a Book descending; they had made the demand: "Show us God openly"; until you show us God, we are not ready to believe that He speaks with you and you are His sent-one. Consequently, they invited their own wretchedness in this way and a thunderbolt seized them. Then, even stranger than that is that they made a calf a deity even after seeing extremely clear miracles. But We overlooked them and granted Moses a clear authority so that no excuse remained for them. We suspended the Mount over their heads at the time of taking the covenant; We instructed them to enter the Tabernacle with humility; We commanded them not to desecrate the Sabbath and took an extremely firm covenant from them for all these points; but they respected no covenant; rather, they broke every covenant, denied Allah's verses, killed the prophets without cause, and said: "The doors of our hearts are closed to your words." They are not closed; rather, Allah has set a seal upon them due to their Kufr, so they will believe only rarely. The purpose of referencing these points is: regarding those whose history is this, whose national temperament is this—what good can be expected from them? Due to these continuous crimes of theirs—the series of which has not broken from the predecessors to the successors—God has cursed them; now no matter what miracle you show them, the thirst for seeing miracles will remain, and the success of faith will never be achieved by them.

Exegesis of Verses 156 to 158: 'wa bi-kufrihim wa qawlihim 'ala Maryama buhtanan 'azhima. wa qawlihim inna qatalnal-masiha 'Isab-na Maryama rasulallah wa ma qataluhu wa ma shalabu-hu wa lakin shubbiha lahum wa innal-ladhina-khtalafu fihi lafi shakkim-minhu ma lahum bihi min 'ilmin illa-ttiba'azh-zhanni wa ma qataluhu yaqina. bar-rafa'ahullahu ilayhi wa kanallahu 'azizun hakima'.

Conjunction of "wa bi-kufrihim": 'wa bi-kufrihim' (and for their disbelief) is conjoined to the preceding sequence of discourse. An incidental phrase had appeared in the middle; after its conclusion, the list of crimes began again:

Nature of the Slander against Maryam: Regarding the "great slander" mentioned in "wa qawlihim 'ala Maryama", we mentioned in the exegesis of Surah Al Imran that the Jews did not have the audacity to place this accusation during the era of Sayyidina Jesus. This is entirely among later inventions. 'inna qatalnal-masiha 'Isab-na Maryama rasulallah' (Indeed, we have killed the Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, the messenger of Allah): the term 'Rasul Allah' is not, in our view, a part of the Jews' statement, but Allah Almighty has clarified the rank of Sayyidina Jesus to highlight the gravity of their crime. 

Purpose of Refuting the Event of the Killing of Jesus: From 'wa ma qataluhu' to 'azizun hakiman' is an incidental phrase. In it, the Jews' claim of killing Jesus was immediately refuted. From this immediate refutation, two aspects come forward. One is that Allah's Messengers are in His protection; God does not allow the tricks of His enemies to succeed against them. For this reason, the Jews' claim that they killed him or crucified him is entirely baseless. They failed completely in this mischief of theirs. However, they became odious and accursed forever by taking the burden of a false claim upon their heads. Second, neither did the event of Jesus's killing occur nor the cross; but the followers of Paul among the Christians took this fictitious myth and authored an entire mythology upon it, and thus 'cut off their own nose on another's omen' (suffered for a false cause).

Meaning of 'wa lakin shubbiha lahum': 'wa lakin shubbiha lahum' (but it was made to appear so to them)—meaning what the Jews wanted to do, they could not do; however, the state of circumstances was made such that they thought they had had Jesus crucified. It is difficult to determine what its form was. Regarding an event about which those who were present at the time had intense disagreement—as proven from the Gospels—trying to determine its form now after two thousand years is merely running after assumptions. The certain point is only as much as the Quran has told: that the Jews could neither kill Sayyidina Jesus nor crucify him, but the matter was made confusing for them. 

Nature of the Event in light of the Gospels: From the narrative of the event present in the Gospels, several points come forward extremely clearly. One, that at that time the political power in the country was with the Romans and they alone were the owners of all political and penal authorities. Second, that the Roman officials and police not only had no interest in crucifying Jesus, but the Roman governor Pilate and other officials were not ready to take the responsibility for this oppression upon themselves in any way. Third, that the circumstances described at the time of arrest and punishment are so tumultuous that in such circumstances, any point could be made believable to the masses, and they could very easily accept a lie as truth and truth as a lie. Fourth, that even after the presumed event of the cross, it is proven from the Gospels that Sayyidina Jesus's disciples saw him. Fifth, that while there was great fame of Sayyidina Jesus's sermons, miracles, and feats, until that time he was neither well-recognized by form among the masses nor did the Roman officials and their police men recognize him. Sixth, that among the Christians too, a group has existed from the beginning that has been of the opinion that the cross was not given to Sayyidina Jesus but was given to someone else entirely, but it was made famous that it was given to him. 

Evidences for all these points exist in the Gospels themselves, and we can gather them very easily, but from that only as much will be proven as the Quran has told: that the matter was made confusing for them. As for the question: what was the form of this confusion? In this chapter, whatever is said will hold the status of nothing more than assumption and conjecture, and we do not like to pursue conjecture. 

Christians 'cut off their own nose on another's omen': 'wa innal-ladhina-khtalafu fihi' (and indeed, those who differ over it) refers to the Christians. Consequently, the fact is that among the Christians too, there is great difference regarding the event itself—as we hinted above—and in the mythology they authored upon it, there are also great differences; and this difference is the natural result of the fact that they based their entire scholasticism (Ilm al-Kalam) upon mere assumption instead of reality, and thus the Christians personally committed suicide by climbing onto that cross from which Allah Almighty protected Sayyidina Jesus.

Taking 'Raising' as merely Elevation of Ranks is contrary to Arabic: 'bar-rafa'ahullahu ilayhi wa kanallahu 'azizun hakima'. We discussed the segment 'rafa'ahullahu ilayhi' in the exegesis of Surah Al Imran. There we also clarified that taking merely the elevation of ranks and degrees from this is contrary to Arabic grammar. Through the reference to the attributes of 'Aziz' and 'Hakim', the objective here is: when God wants to do a task, He is dominant over His intent. No path is closed for Him. He opens a path through His strategy and wisdom from wherever He wills.

The Original Sequence of Discourse: It should be noted that the original issue under discussion in these verses is not the refutation of Sayyidina Jesus's killing or cross, or their rejection or acceptance. This point, as we hinted, has appeared merely as an incidental point in the middle of the sequence of discourse. The original sequence of discourse running from 'yas'aluka ahlul-kitab' is this: the excuses the People of the Book are making for not believing in the Quran and the Messengership of Muhammad (PBUH) are all miracles of that 'evil habit' which has been in their nature since the beginning. They saw miracles, each greater than the last, but no miracle could satisfy them. They always committed covenant-breaking, always belied, and faced every truth with stubbornness and arrogance, pride and rebellion. To the extent that they slandered Maryam and are themselves claimants of killing God's Messenger, the Messiah son of Mary. When the point reached here, the refutation of the event of the killing and the cross was made incidentally, and simultaneously the Christians were also warned: that they too mindlessly accepted this lie as truth and built the palace of their entire scholasticism upon it.

'wa im-min ahlil-kitabi illa layu'minanna bihi qabla mawtihi wa yawmal-qiyamati yakunu 'alayhim shahida'.

'wa im-min ahlil-kitab' refers to both Jews and Christians: the style of expression 'wa im-min ahlil-kitab' is showing generalization—meaning it consists of both groups, Jews and Christians. Although the mention of the Jews had been continuing from above, since in the incidental phrase—as we hinted—the mention of the Christians' folly had also appeared, therefore the occasion for referencing them also arose; and what is described here relates to both groups as a group, not as individuals.

The word "Iman" in the meaning of 'being certain': in 'layu'minanna', the 'Lam' is for emphasis and oath, and the word 'Iman' here is in the meaning of 'being certain/convinced.' The faith (Iman) authoritative in religion is only that which consists of all three components: certainty, confirmation, and acknowledgment. Apart from this, there is a faith in which the components of certainty and confirmation are not found but the component of expression and acknowledgment is found—this is the faith of the hypocrites. Similarly, there is also a faith in which certainty is found but the components of confirmation and acknowledgment are missing—this is the faith of the arrogant and rebellious. The being-truth of the truth becomes perfectly clear to them, but due to their vanity and mischief, they avoid its confirmation and acknowledgment and try to hide this mischief of theirs behind the veils of various excuses. Surah An-Naml mentions this group: 'And they rejected them, while their inner selves were convinced [way-tayqanat-ha anfusuhum] of them, out of injustice and haughtiness.' Under this also falls that faith which Pharaoh brought at the time of drowning. Although all components of faith were present in his faith, the faith brought after the water has passed over the head holds no rank in religion. In the verse under discussion, in our view, as we submitted, 'Iman' refers to certainty.

Referent of the Pronoun in "layu'minanna bihi": in 'layu'minanna bihi' and 'qabla mawtihi', the referent of the first pronoun in our view is the Holy Quran and the referent of the second is the Holy Prophet (PBUH). The meaning is: today these People of the Book appoint this condition for acknowledging the truthfulness of the Quran and the Prophet—that they show a Book descending from heaven, then they will be certain that the Quran is in reality Allah's Book and Muhammad (PBUH) is truly Allah's Messenger; and through this pretext, they are ignoring all those rational, transmitted, natural, and historical arguments present in proof of the Quran's and the Prophet's truthfulness; but that time too is not far when these Jews and Christians will see every single point said by the Quran and the Prophet with their own eyes in the form of events, and the disgrace, failure, humiliation, and defeat the Quran is giving news of for them will come before their eyes before the Prophet departs from the world in such a way that beling it will not be possible for them, even though the success of faith will not be obtained by them even after seeing all this.

This is not mere News but a Threat: It should be noted here that this sentence is an oath; therefore, it is not correct to take it in the meaning of a mere declarative sentence. It does not only mean that before the Prophet departs from the world, these people will become certain of the Quran; rather, threat and warning are also within it. Meaning: the points being explained to them today through argument but which do not enter their understanding—what will they do tomorrow when they come before them in the form of events? At that time, they will nonetheless have to accept even if they continue to deny with their tongues a million times.

Opinion of Ikrimah: It is worth remembering here that among the predecessors, Ikrimah considers the referent of the first pronoun to be the Holy Prophet (PBUH), but generally people did not give importance to this statement due to the distance created by a long incidental phrase, even though the distance created by an incidental phrase is not worth considering. In such a situation, one has to respect the sequence of discourse while turning a blind eye to it.

The Prophet's (PBUH) Testimony in the World and the Hereafter: 'wa yawmal-qiyamati yakunu 'alayhim shahida' (and on the Day of Resurrection he will be a witness over them): this is the mention of that testimony which the Prophet (PBUH) will give on the Day of Resurrection over all those people over whom you (PBUH) have given testimony of the true religion in this world. The mention of this testimony passed in verse 41 of this Surah and we discussed it with clarification there. Although all prophets will give this testimony over their respective nations—consequently, the mention of the testimony Sayyidina Jesus will give also occurred in Al-Ma'idah verses 116-117—nonetheless, the Holy Prophet (PBUH) since he is the Seal of the Prophets and you (PBUH) clarified to the Jews, Christians, and Arabs all what Allah's original religion is, therefore it is your position alone that you tell on the Day of Resurrection what you told the people on behalf of Allah, and through this testimony of yours, the proof will be established over the people.

This sentence too, in our view, carries the same threat and warning that the first sentence carries. Meaning: the truthfulness and being-right of the Prophet (PBUH) will become clear to these People of the Book in this world too in such a way that no room for denial will remain, and in the Hereafter too you (PBUH) will testify that you had well-warned them of every single misguidance of the Jews and Christians; if they remained in these same misguidances even after these warnings, it is their own responsibility; the proof had been completed over them.

160-161: 'fa-bi-zhulmim-minal-ladhina hadu harramna 'alayhim tayyibatin uhillat lahum wa bi-shaddihim 'an sabilillahi kathira. wa akh-dhihimur-riba wa qad nuhu 'anhu wa aklihim amwalan-nasi bil-batili wa a'tadna lil-kafirina minhum 'adhaban alima'.

Nature of the Prohibition of Lawful things upon the Jews: In 'fa-bi-zhulmim-minal-ladhina hadu harramna 'alayhim', from the precedence of the word 'Zhulm', the point is produced that those lawful things which became prohibited upon the Jews—this too occurred because of their committing oppression against their own souls; Allah committed no injustice against them. The forms of lawful things being made prohibited among them were discussed by us in the exegesis of Al-Baqarah and also under Al Imran verse 50.

Command of Usury's Prohibition in Leviticus: 'wa akh-dhihimur-riba wa qad nuhu 'anhu' (and their taking of usury while they had been forbidden from it): the explicit command of usury's prohibition can be seen in Leviticus 25:35-38. The meaning of 'wa aklihim amwalan-nasi bil-batil' was discussed under verse 29 of this Surah.

The Jews' Original Obstacles to Faith: These points also relate to the same indictment of the Jews that passed above. The meaning is: the things preventing them from believing in the Quran and the Prophet are in reality these crimes of theirs, not that you are not showing them some scripture descending from heaven. They have always been stiff-necked and disobedient. Through their stiff-neckedness alone, they had lawful things made prohibited upon themselves; and due to their stiff-neckedness alone, they always became a hurdle in the path of truth; and it is this very thing that made them ready to make usury lawful even though they were explicitly prohibited from it in the Torah; and because of it, they opened all other doors of unlawful consumption even though they were commissioned to close those doors. Regarding those whose character is this—how do you expect from them that they will believe in the Quran even after seeing the greatest miracle? It stated: for as many of them as have become firm on Kufr, We have prepared a painful punishment.

'lakinir-rasikhuna fil-'ilmi minhum wal-mu'minuna yu'minuna bi-ma unzila ilayka wa ma unzila min qablika wal-muqiminash-shalata wal-mu'tunaz-zakata wal-mu'minuna billahi wal-yawmil-akhiri ula'ika sanu'tihim ajran 'azhima'.

'Rasikhun fil-Ilm' refers to the Godly Scholars of the People of the Book: 'lakinir-rasikhuna fil-'ilmi minhum wal-mu'minuna'. 'Rasikhun fil-Ilm' (those firm in knowledge) refers to those scholars whose feet were well-anchored in the knowledge of the Sharia, who were firm in both creed and action, and whose character and morals were established on the straight path from every aspect. Scholars were not few among the Jews—they were countless—but the Sharia held the status of merely a showy garment for them which they wore to go out into the markets and among the masses. This knowledge had no connection with their thought and sight or their heart and soul. These scholars were not only partners with their nation in all those crimes mentioned above, but they were their mentors in them; for this reason, if anything could be expected from them, it was only that they lead their nation in opposing the Quran and the Prophet (PBUH). However, those holy souls among them who were acquainted with the taste of the wisdom of religion—their feet were firm in the knowledge of the Sharia. For this reason, they were blessed with success from Allah Almighty to stand against the storm of their nation's unanimous opposition. Here, to distinguish these holy souls from the general Jewish scholars, they were honored with the title of "Rasikhun fil-Ilm" instead of just scholars. Meaning: they are not ones to fly in the direction of the wind like the debris of a storm, but are ones to remain firm on their stance of truth like rocks.

"Al-Mu'minun" refers to Sound-Natured and God-Fearing Individuals: 'Al-Mu'minun' refers to those sound-natured people who, although they did not hold the rank of 'Rasikhun fil-Ilm', were nonetheless distinguished in the entire society in terms of the soundness of their nature, the capability of their heart, and the purity of their character; and despite the general corruption of the Jews, they remained firm on God's guidance and Sharia, and when the call of Islam fell upon their ears, they stepped forward to accept it too. 'yu'minuna bi-ma unzila ilayka wa ma unzila min qablika' (they believe in what was revealed to you and what was revealed before you): this is the predicate (khabar)—meaning these 'Rasikhun fil-Ilm' scholars and sound-natured people believe in this Quran too and hold faith in the Book before it too. This is that group of the People of the Book whose praise the Quran has made in various places. The mention of this group also passed under Al Imran verse 113.

The Style of 'wal-muqiminash-shalat' and its Benefits: 'wal-muqiminash-shalat' (and especially those who establish prayer) is conjoined to 'Al-Mu'minun'. But it has become 'mansub' (accusative) according to that rule of eloquent Arabic which our grammarians describe with the terminology 'ala sabil al-ikhtishash' (by way of specification) or 'ala sabil al-madh' (by way of praise). Examples of this are abundantly present in the poetry of Arab poets. The literal effect of this shift in style on the listener is that this variety alerts them to the word, and the semantic benefit is that merely through the shift in style—without adding a single letter—the subject of specification and praise is produced within it. For example: if this word were 'wal-muqimunash-shalat' according to its general style, its meaning would only be "and the establishers of prayer"; but when the style was changed to 'wal-muqiminash-shalat', its meaning became "and especially the establishers of prayer." Through which both the extraordinary praise and the specificity of these described ones were clarified, as well as that importance and greatness prayer holds in the system of religion. An extremely fine example of this style has also passed in Surah Al-Baqarah. There it stated: '...and those who fulfill their promise when they promise and [especially] the patient in poverty and hardship and during battle.' Here too, see that 'as-sabirin' is conjoined to 'al-mufuna'; in this regard, it was meant to be 'as-sabiruna', but it became 'as-sabirina' according to the same rule to which we referenced above.

The Rank of Patience and Prayer in the Sharia: To understand the secret of the wisdom of religion here—what rank patience and prayer hold in the Sharia—read the exegesis of the verse 'seek help through patience and prayer' which passed in Al-Baqarah, so that the reason for mentioning these two things with this care and emphasis is well-understood. As far as patience is concerned: it is an open reality that the person who does not possess the attribute of patience—the building of their religion is entirely on sand; it can easily fall from any jolt. As for prayer: in reality, this is the thing that consistently reminds of the covenant the servant makes with their Lord; for this reason, the person who wastes it will eventually lose the entire religion. Regarding the Jews, it was described in the Quran that they completely wasted the prayer ('adha'ush-shalat'), the result of which was that they lost the entire religion. Among them, only those people remained firm on the religion who remained firm on the prayer, and these are the people who eventually became the acceptors of Islam's call too. The mention of this group occurred in Al Imran thus: 'They are not [all] the same; among the People of the Book is a community standing [in obedience], reciting the verses of Allah during periods of the night and prostrating [in prayer].' 'wal-mu'minuna billahi wal-yawmil-akhir' is exactly like verse 4 of Al-Baqarah: 'and of the Hereafter they are certain.' Just as there, after the mention of 'believing in the unseen' and other attributes, the need for this segment apparently did not remain, yet He mentioned it to highlight the certainty of the Hereafter—which is the original driver of all goodness and Taqwa—similarly here too, apparently after 'wal-mu'minun', there was no need for its repetition, yet He repeated it for the reminder of the Hereafter. This is because in religion, all life and movement is from faith in the Hereafter alone.

Subject of Verses 163 to 175: Further on, addressing the Prophet (PBUH), consolation was first given: do not care for these opponents who today do not believe in the revelation sent down to you but are demanding an open Book descended from heaven. Neither is revelation an unknown thing in the world whose experience occurred only to you, nor is Prophethood and Messengership such an alien thing whose expression was made only by you. Revelation too has come into the world before you, and Messengers too have come in countless numbers; if people are repelled by such a recognized thing, and for whose testing and weighing so many standards and touchstones exist, people are refusing to recognize it—then the fault is not yours but their own. For your satisfaction, this thing suffices: that the testimony of Allah and His angels is in your favor. As for these people who have become firm on Kufr and opposition—they have wronged their own souls, and due to that, they are no longer capable of the path of faith and guidance opening for them. Now only the path of Hell remains for them. After that, addressing people generally and the Christians specifically, He warned: value the 'Clear Light' (Nur Mubin) Allah has revealed in the form of the Quran for the guidance of creation, and come to the path of guidance while abandoning the path of misguidance; otherwise, remember that those who turn away and deny it will face an extremely painful punishment. Now recite the verses.

'inna awhayna ilayka kama awhayna ila Nuhin wan-nabiyyina mim-ba'dihi wa awhayna ila Ibrahima wa Isma'ila wa Ishaqa wa Ya'quba wal-asbati wa 'Isa wa Ayyuba wa Yunusa wa Haruna wa Sulaymana wa atayna Dawuda Zabura. wa rusulan qad qashashnahum 'alayka min qablu wa rusulan-lam naqshush-hum 'alayka wa kallamallahu Musa taklima'.

Discussion on the word "Asbat" passed in Surah Al-Baqarah. 

Zabur (Psalms): A scripture by the name of "Zabur" consisting of the prayers and supplications of Sayyidina David (PBUH) is included in the collection of the Torah. In the Quran, its name has also appeared in the definite form. Here, in the indefinite form, it is in my view for 'magnification of status' (tafkhim-e-shan), which clarifies the importance of the Zabur. Although it is correct to keep the current Zabur in the same rank as other scriptures of the Torah—it contains defects of translation and the specific possibility of addition and subtraction—nonetheless, read it and the breast becomes filled with the light of faith and trust.

Nature of the Divine Dialogue with Sayyidina Moses (PBUH): We discussed 'wa kallamallahu Musa taklima' elsewhere. Both the Torah and the Quran prove that Sayyidina Moses (PBUH) holds this distinction: Allah Almighty blessed him with His specific address and speech, and the glory of this address and speech was different from the revelation with which other prophets were honored. Although it is proven from both the Quran and Torah that this address and speech too was not face-to-face with God but 'from behind a veil' (min wara'i hijab).

Nature of the Order in the Prophets' Names: The order of the prophets' names listed here is historical from Prophet Noah to the mention of Prophet Jacob and his offspring, but after that, the order has become 'attributive' (sifati). Sayyidina Jesus, Sayyidina Job (Ayyub), Sayyidina Jonah (Yunus), and Sayyidina Aaron (Harun) share in a specific type of trial and a specific type of Divine support. Sayyidina Solomon and Sayyidina David are both prophets and both kings. The reason for bringing the mention of Sayyidina David (PBUH) after Sayyidina Solomon (PBUH) is to draw attention with special care to the Zabur. At the very end is the mention of Sayyidina Moses because the Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Sayyidina Moses are 'similar prophets.' The mention of this point is in both the Quran and Hadiths. 

Objective of the Mention of Prophets: Although not all prophets are mentioned here, the variety in terms of attributes alongside the commonality among them has been highlighted, and if any of them attained some specificity and distinction in terms of revelation, address, and speech, that too has come forward. Through this entire reference, the Quran's objective is: these are the prophets whose names and works were also described in the scriptures of the Torah, and this has been the method by which Allah blessed these prophets with His revelation and His address and speech. The People of the Book are aware of all of them; is there any mention in them of Allah revealing a Book to some prophet such that everyone saw it descending? With Moses (PBUH), Allah Almighty indeed spoke, in the way speech is performed, but the Jews' satisfaction was not achieved even by that; they raised this doubt regarding that too: until God speaks with us face-to-face, how can we believe that He speaks with you and you are His sent-one? What is the remedy for such patients of doubt?

The aspect of Argumentation in these Verses: The aspect of argumentation in these verses, as we hinted, is: if revelation were some such thing whose experience only you had presented, and Prophethood and Messengership were some such thing whose claim only you had made in the world, then to an extent there was room to consider those people excused who expressed anxiety at your claim and did not accept this unique claim until they satisfied themselves from every aspect; but when a long chain of prophets exists and the registers of the forms and points in which revelation came to them also exist, then what is the meaning of these baseless demands for proof of your truthfulness from those who are name-bearers of all these Messengers and prophets and claimants of being bearers of all the Books revealed to these prophets? Just as every group has some shared characteristics and attributes, similarly the group of prophets also has shared characteristics and attributes; these are so prominent that their bearers appear separate from the entire world. Neither is it possible for any false claimant to enter among them, nor is there the possibility that one included among them be separated from their category.

'rusulam-mubash-shirina wa munzhirina li-alla yakuna lin-nasi 'alallahi hujjatumb-ba'dar-rusul wa kanallahu 'azizun hakima'.

'Rusulan' can be 'mansub' (accusative) from an omitted verb and can also be an appositive (badal). In both forms, there will be no specific difference in terms of meaning.

The Shared Mission of the Prophets: In this verse, the shared mission of the Messengers was also told, as well as the necessity that called for their sending. Their shared mission is to give glad tidings to people and to inform them of danger. Glad tidings of the fact that those who adopt the life of faith and righteous action—for them are the joys and successes of eternal life. Awareness of the fact that those who adopt the path of Kufr and bad deeds—for them is the eternal punishment of the Fire.

The Necessity of the Prophets' Advent: Its necessity was told thus: so that Allah's proof is completed over the people. The opportunity should not remain for anyone to say that no one came to inform them of this danger, otherwise they would never have adopted the path of Kufr and bad deeds. Although God is Mighty (Aziz)—He could have punished people for their disobediences even without sending prophets and no one could have caught His hand—He is also Wise (Hakim); the demand of His wisdom was that He gives punishment to someone only after the finality of proof (itmam al-hujjah). This proof was completed after the arrival of the Messengers. The testimony of reason and nature—which was deposited within the human regarding faith in Allah, faith in the Hereafter, and love for goodness and justice, and hatred for oppression and sin—these prophets thoroughly revealed it. Whatever its demands could be were also described in full detail. Now if someone stumbles, it means they stumble in the full light of day while possessing both eyes. 

It should be remembered here that human nature is not deprived of the consciousness of good and evil, nor is reason incapable of distinguishing between truth and falsehood. Allah Almighty created the human upon the 'best of forms' (ahsan-e-taqwim) and blessed them with the recognition of good and evil; but the demand of His mercy was that He bless them with the guidance of revelation and prophets alongside the guidance of reason and nature, so that the demands of reason and nature manifest perfectly clearly before them and not even a minor excuse remains for misguidance.

166-169: 'lakinillahu yash-hadu bi-ma anzala ilayka anzalahu bi-'ilmih wal-mala'ikatu yash-hadun wa kafa billahi shahida. innal-ladhina kafaru wa shaddu 'an sabilillahi qad dhallu dhalalam-ba'ida. innal-ladhina kafaru wa zhalamu lam yakunillahu liyagh-fira lahum wa la liyah-diyahum thariqa. illa thariqa jahannama khalidina fiha abada wa kana dhalika 'alallahi yasira'.

The aspect of 'Istidrak' (Correction): The particle "Lakin" (But) is for 'Istidrak'. This 'Istidrak' is upon the point that emerges from the segment above. The detailed reference to the chain of prophets and the sending of revelation to them above aimed, as we submitted, to clarify to the beliers among the People of the Book: regarding a reality that is so well-recognized, and for whose testing and weighing so many standards and touchstones exist—if they belie it, then their wretchedness has truly arrived. Following this, by performing 'Istidrak' upon it, He stated: if they belie it, let them belie it; Allah nonetheless bears witness to what He revealed to you—He revealed it with His knowledge; meaning the testimony to the fact that this is from Allah, the soul and Satan have no role in it, Allah revealed it with His knowledge, no mixture of any psychological whisper or any satanic doubt exists in it, its connection is with the pure fountainhead of Divine knowledge, and it is entirely pure and unmixed like the water of life. Furthermore, He stated: the testimony of Allah alone is sufficient in its favor, but along with Allah, the angels too are its witnesses. 

This Speech is for the Prophet's Consolation: This instruction, as is clear, is for the Prophet's (PBUH) consolation: if these opponents do not accept your revelation as revelation, let them not; its correctness and truthfulness do not depend on their accepting or not. For your satisfaction, this suffices: that God and the angels are its witnesses. The assembly of holiness with which you are connected—for you the authority is their authority, not that of these deprived people who are incapable of distinguishing a pearl. These people, by becoming distant from the truth, have become so distant that now no possibility remains for them to turn toward the path of truth. Through their Kufr and the oppression they committed against their own souls with their own hands, they have not even left themselves capable of God forgiving them or showing them any path other than the path of Hell. Now they will remain in Hell forever. And for God, this matter is very easy. He completed the proof over them; now this thing will not be at all heavy upon Him that they fall into Hell and remain in it forever. We discussed the segment 'wa kana dhalika 'alallahi yasira' elsewhere too.

An Internal Proof of the Prophet's Truthfulness: This speech, although in the place of consolation as we hinted, is also a proof of the revelation's truthfulness which relates to the person of the Prophet. The proof of the revelation's truthfulness described above is of the nature of historical testimony. Meaning: by testing on the touchstone of the history of prophets and their revelation, the rank of the Quran and the Prophet was determined. Now this is a second proof described, whose nature is that of an internal proof. Its aim is: the Prophet hears, understands, and tests the testimony of God and the angels within his own interior in such a way that no room for any doubt remains for him regarding his revelation's truthfulness. This type of testimony is not obtained by any non-prophet; for this reason, there is a 'heaven and earth' difference between the inspiration (ilham) of a non-prophet and the revelation (wahi) of a prophet. It is possible that a person considers their own state as inspiration when it is merely a psychological or satanic whisper; but the revelation upon a prophet comes from such an horizon, comes with such force and power, and comes with such support and testimony of Allah and the angels that it is in itself such a 'Burhan' (proof) after which no room for doubt remains. It is this very aspect of the revelation's truthfulness due to which even if the entire creation belies the prophet, nonetheless no difference is produced in his confidence. His assembly and gathering are within his own interior where he possesses the companionship of God and the Holy Spirit. Detailed discussion on this issue will occur in Surah At-Tahrim, God willing.

A General Warning in which the Focus is toward the Christians: Although the address is general, from the next verse it is estimated that the focus of the speech is toward the People of the Book, especially the Christians. It stated: O people! Allah's Messenger has come to you with the truth from your Lord. The mixtures you had made in Allah's religion—due to which it had become impossible to know what the truth is—now, being purified from all those mixtures, the religion has come to you anew in its perfect form. Believe in it; in it is your own betterment. If you deny it, then remember that God will not be harmed at all. You alone will be harmed. God is independent of all. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him. All are in His control, and He is aware of everyone's deeds; He will give every soul the reward for its deeds. If this thing is being delayed today, it does not mean there is no reward/punishment. Rather, it is being delayed under His wisdom. God is also Wise.

'ya-ahlal-kitabi la taghlu fi dinikum wa la taqulu 'alallahi illal-haqqa innamal-masihu 'Isab-na Maryama rasulullahi wa kalimatuhu alqaha ila Maryama wa ruhum-minhu fa-aminu billahi wa rusulihi wa la taqulu thalathatun-intahu khayral-lakum innama-llahu ilahuw-wahidun subhanahu ay-yakuna lahu waladun lahu ma fis-samawati wa ma fil-ardhi wa kafa billahi wakila'.

Meaning of "Ghuluw" (Excess): 'la taghlu fi dinikum'. "Ghala yaghlu" means to increase, to exceed, to overstep; when this word comes in relation to religion, its meaning is that whatever rank, status, or weight and position a thing has in religion, it is increased to become something else. What is a quarter-seer is made a maund; a command that holds only the rank of recommendation and preference is given the rank of obligation and necessity; a person who is a jurist or Mujtahid or Companion is made an infallible Imam; one whom Allah made a prophet and messenger is made a partner of God or God himself; one whose only veneration is required, their worship is started. These and all points of this caliber enter into 'Ghuluw', and just as 'Tafreet' (neglect) is a very great crime in matters of religion, similarly this 'Ifraat' (excess) is a very great Fitna. Through it, that temperament of the religion—which is entirely moderation—is completely disrupted, and its Divine composition and arrangement—which makes its components a captivating embodiment of beauty and grace—is entirely distorted. Although all people of religions have fallen into this 'Ghuluw'—to the extent that we Muslims too, who were most emphasized to remain firm on justice and equity, fell into this Fitna—nonetheless, consider that the Christians hold the rank of 'Imamate' in this corruption. Their original disease is precisely this: that they distorted the appearance of the entire religion due to this 'Ghuluw' of theirs. Sayyidina Jesus (PBUH) was a servant of God and His messenger. They made him the Son of God and then took him and seated him on the throne of divinity; Maryam (PBUH) was Sayyidina Jesus's mother, they made her—God forbid—the Mother of God; Gabriel is a servant of God and an angel, they gave him too the status of a 'person' (hypostasis) and associated him in the Trinity of divinity; Sayyidina Jesus emphasized avoiding the ornaments of the world and worldly life, so they set up an entire system of monasticism. In short, at the hands of this 'Ghuluw', they left nothing of the religion that remained in its place. The thing of the floor reached the throne, and the thing of the throne came to the floor. 

The Path of the Fitna of 'Ghuluw': 'wa la taqulu 'alallahi illal-haqq' (and do not say about Allah except the truth). The path through which the Fitna of 'Ghuluw' in religion is produced—and then from where the equipment for its support and strengthening is provided—this is its blockage. Obviously, if only that point is attributed to Allah which He has stated, then no door of Fitna can open from it. The door of Fitna opens at the time when that point is attributed to Him which He has not stated. This very thing is 'Bid'ah' (innovation), and it is from here that Satan finds the path to enter the religion and create corruption in it. The door from which the Christians were destroyed is this very door. They made all the absurdities of Paul a part of their religion and then built the palace of their entire scholasticism upon it. It is worth remembering that in the Gospels, despite the alterations, not much material exists for the decoration of this Fitna in which the Christians became afflicted. Even if some things were entered into them through the path of alteration, the material for their refutation—as we clarified in Al Imran—is also present in them. The original material of misguidance is in Paul's teachings, and they have no connection to God or the Messiah. 

The Original Reality of the Messiah: 'innamal-masihu 'Isab-na Maryama...' (The Messiah, Jesus, son of Mary, was but...). now that correct point regarding the Messiah was told which Allah has stated regarding him. It is that he is Allah's Messenger, his birth occurred from Allah's word "Kun" which Allah Almighty inspired toward Maryam, and he was also granted a spirit from God's side; the meaning is that his birth contrary to habit is not something on the basis of which he be given the status of divinity. His birth occurred from God's word "Kun" just as Adam's birth occurred from the word "Kun", and God breathed a spirit into him just as He breathed a spirit into Adam. Causes/means are merely the veil of the manifest; existence and life are received by whoever receives them only from God's command and from the spirit He bestows. This discussion also passed under Al Imran verse 59. 

'fa-aminu billahi wa rusulihi wa la taqulu thalathah'. Meaning: repenting from this Trinity maze you have established by making a few of your superstitions the foundation, believe in Allah and His messengers. Through the words "believe in the messengers," a hint was made toward the reality: when the teaching of all God's messengers exists in the Torah and Gospel, and in this teaching from the Messiah back to Adam nowhere is a trace of the Trinity found, then after all, from where did you fabricate this Fitna? So the correct path is that instead of carving out this separate side-path, you too walk on that straight path upon which all prophets and messengers walked. After all, how is it possible that the Messiah's teaching be entirely separate from the unanimous teaching of Allah and His messengers? 

The Creed of the Trinity: "Thalathah" (Three) refers to the Christians' creed of the Trinity, which is among Paul's inventions. According to this creed, the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit are all three partners in divinity. This creed is entirely molded in the mold of the polytheists, but simultaneously the effort has also been made to maintain some 'honor' for the teaching of Tawheed given in extremely clear words in the Gospels. 

1. Our respected Maulana Abdul Majid Daryabadi has quoted this creed of the Christians in their own words in his Tafseer thus: "The divinity of the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit is one. Glory equal, eternal majesty the same; as the Father is, so is the Son and so is the Holy Spirit. The Father uncreated, the Son uncreated, and the Holy Spirit uncreated; the Father infinite, the Son infinite, and the Holy Spirit infinite; the Father eternal, the Son eternal, and the Holy Spirit eternal. Nonetheless, not three eternals but one eternal. Thus not three infinites and not three uncreateds but one uncreated and one infinite. Likewise the Father Almighty, the Son Almighty, and the Holy Spirit Almighty. Yet not three Almighties but one Almighty. So the Father God, the Son God, and the Holy Spirit God, yet not three Gods but one God."

In 'intahu khayral-lakum' (Desist - it is better for you), 'khayran' is 'mansub' (accusative) from an omitted verb just as in the previous verse. This point here is in the style of a threat. Meaning: come out of this cycle of 'three in one and one in three', otherwise wretchedness will arrive. Allah alone is the One Deity. There is no partner in His divinity. It is entirely contrary to His attributes of divinity that children be attributed to Him. He is Eternal and Everlasting and independent and indifferent to all. Whatever is in the heavens and the earth belongs to Him alone, so what need does He have for sons and daughters? He is sufficient for everyone's design, everyone's help, and everyone's trust; so what is the meaning of joining another with Him? Meaning: either God possesses some void in His Being, then room for Shirk is produced; or some lack exists within Him in terms of others' needs, then room for it is produced; if He is perfect in His Being and Attributes and sufficient and enough for His creation, then from where did the room for Shirk emerge?

172-173: 'lay-yastankifal-masihu ay-yakuna 'abdallillahi wa lal-mala'ikatul-muqarrabun wa may-yastankif 'an 'ibadatihi wa yastakbir fa-sayah-shuruhum ilayhi jami'a. fa-ammal-ladhina amanu wa 'amilush-shalihati fa-yuwaffihim ujurahum wa yaziduhum-min fadhlih wa ammal-ladhinas-tankafu wastakbaru fa-yu'adh-dhibuhum 'adhaban alima wa la yajiduna lahum-min dunillahi waliyyaw-wa la nashira'.

"Istinkaf" means to turn away from something due to pride, honor, self-respect, or arrogance. 

To understand the correct force of this verse, keep in sight the verse 'la taghlu fi dinikum'. The major cause of becoming afflicted with the Fitna of 'Ghuluw' (excess) is in reality 'Istikbar' (arrogance). Those who believe in something or someone—if they are not aware of limits or are not observers of them—their desire and effort is to prove that thing or person as greater than all things and all persons. Then they begin increasing it according to their arrogance until they increase it to reach that limit where the arrogance is satisfied: that now in the field of superiority, no rival of theirs remains and here no one can challenge them. This very Fitna faced the Christians. When they believed in Sayyidina Jesus, they could not suffice with merely believing in him as a servant of Allah and His messenger. They thought: Allah's servants and messengers are many; if the Messiah too is only a servant of Allah and a messenger, then what is the distinction of him and his believers? This motive—which is open arrogance—incited them to try to prove him a partner of God through stretching and pulling. 

The Quran, keeping this very mindset of the Christians in view, stated: the Messiah never considered it a shame to be a servant of God, nor will he; nor will the Holy Spirit and other close angels consider it a shame. They well know their rank and status. However, those who considered God's servitude a shame and triggered all this corruption in the intoxication of their arrogance—God will gather all such people before Him. On that Day, those who adopted the path of faith and righteous action—Allah will give them their full reward and also bless them from His favor. As for those who considered God's servitude a shame and made a 'mountain out of a molehill' (bat ka batangadh) out of pride—Allah will give them a painful punishment and there will be no designer or helper for them who could stand against Allah on their behalf.

174-175: 'ya-ayyu-han-nasu qad ja'akum burhanum-mir-rabbikum wa anzalna ilaykum nuram-mubina. fa-ammal-ladhina amanu billahi wa'tashamu bihi fa-sayudkhiluhum fi rahmatim-minhu wa fadhliw-wa yahdihim ilayhi siratam-mustaqima'.

Meaning of "Burhan" and "Nur Mubin": "Burhan" (Proof) and "Nur Mubin" (Clear Light) refer to the Holy Quran. Through the word "Burhan", its rational and argumentative aspect was clarified: that it is a decisive proof; within it exists a silencing and satisfying answer to every doubt, every objection, and every question, provided a person reflects on it with an open heart. Through "Nur Mubin", its practical aspect was clarified: that it guides toward the straight path by clarifying truth and falsehood in all the ups and downs of life and brings one out of darkness into light. 

"Hidayat" refers to the Guidance toward the Sought-after Goal: In 'wa yahdihim ilayhi siratam-mustaqima', the word 'Hidayat' (guidance) is in my view for that guidance which the believers will obtain in the Hereafter. The Quran has used this word in this meaning in multiple places. Discussion on the various stages of guidance passed in Al-Baqarah. The guidance of the Hereafter is guidance toward the sought-after and desired goal. The point that this guidance relates to the Hereafter emerges from its conjunction with 'fa-sayudkhiluhum' (He will admit them), which relates explicitly to the Hereafter; and the point that this guidance is toward the sought-after goal emerges from the word "ilayhi" (to Him). Meaning: those who believe in Allah and hold fast to this 'Rope of Allah' which has been revealed to them in the form of the Quran—God will bless them with His infinite mercy and favor and will also guide them directly and straightforwardly toward His proximity; and this will be the greatest of the blessings of the Hereafter because the original goal of all guidance and Sharia and the original sought-after goal of all the believers' efforts is this very Divine proximity. The address of this verse is general, in which Muslims, People of the Book, and Arabs all are included.

Subject of Verse 176:
Final Verse as an Appendix: The Surah ended at the previous verse. Now, one verse has been attached as an appendix which was revealed later to clarify a specific issue of the inheritance laws described at the start of the Surah. At its end, through the words "Thus Allah makes clear," He also hinted that this is an explanatory verse which was revealed later as a clarification. An example of this type of appendix has already passed in Surah Al-Baqarah. Recite the verse.

'yastaftunaka qulillahu yuftikum fil-kalalah in-imru'un halaka laysa lahu waladuw-wa lahu ukhtun fa-laha nishfu ma taraka wa huwa yarithuha il-lam yakul-laha walad fa-in kanata-thnatayni fa-lahumath-thuluthani mimma taraka wa in kanu ikhwatar-rijalaw-wa nisa'an fa-lidhdhakari mithlu hazh-zhil-unthayayn yubayyinullahu lakum an tadhillu wallahu bi-kulli shay'in 'alim'.

Ruling for the Inheritance of 'Kalalah': The ruling for the inheritance of 'Kalalah' passed in verse 12. 'Kalalah' refers to that deceased person who has neither roots (parents) nor branches (children), but only brothers and sisters, etc. If the ruling of verse 12 is accepted as specific only to uterine (maternal) sisters, then after this explanatory ruling, every aspect of the ruling for the inheritance of 'Kalalah' becomes clear. Its details are present in the books of Fiqh and Fara'id (inheritance law).

These are the final lines of the exegesis of Surah An-Nisa which were committed to paper by the pen of this sinner. May Allah Almighty forgive the slips and create room in hearts for the correct points. And our final call is that all praise is due to Allah, the Lord of the worlds.
